```text
     1‚Üípackage cursorcli
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"bytes"
     5‚Üí	"encoding/hex"
     6‚Üí	"encoding/json"
     7‚Üí	"log/slog"
     8‚Üí	"strings"
     9‚Üí)
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí// Protocol Buffer constants for Cursor's blob reference encoding
    12‚Üíconst (
    13‚Üí	// Protocol Buffer field tags for blob references
    14‚Üí	pbFieldTag1 = 0x0a // Protocol Buffer field 1 tag (wire type 2: length-delimited)
    15‚Üí	pbFieldTag2 = 0x12 // Protocol Buffer field 2 tag (wire type 2: length-delimited)
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí	// Blob ID encoding
    18‚Üí	pbBlobIDLength = 0x20 // Length byte indicating 32-byte blob ID follows
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí	// Blob ID validation
    21‚Üí	minNonPrintableBytes = 8 // Minimum non-printable bytes to consider valid blob ID (vs text data)
    22‚Üí)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí// CursorBlob represents a blob with its references and content
    25‚Üítype CursorBlob struct {
    26‚Üí	RowID      int             `json:"rowid"`
    27‚Üí	ID         string          `json:"id"`
    28‚Üí	Data       json.RawMessage `json:"data"`
    29‚Üí	RawData    []byte          `json:"-"` // Original binary data, not included in JSON output
    30‚Üí	References []string        `json:"-"` // IDs of blobs this blob references, not included in JSON output
    31‚Üí}
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí// parseReferences extracts blob ID references from binary data.
    34‚Üí// Cursor uses Protocol Buffer encoding where references to other blobs are stored
    35‚Üí// as length-delimited fields with 32-byte blob IDs. This creates a directed acyclic
    36‚Üí// graph (DAG) structure that represents the conversation flow and message dependencies.
    37‚Üífunc parseReferences(data []byte, currentBlobID string, currentRowID int) []string {
    38‚Üí	var references []string
    39‚Üí	seenRefs := make(map[string]bool) // Track to avoid duplicates
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí	// Search the entire blob for references
    42‚Üí	// While this could potentially have false positives in message content,
    43‚Üí	// the entropy check should filter most of them out and the chance of
    44‚Üí	// false positives matching actual message IDs is astronomically low, so
    45‚Üí	// in practice any false positives will have no impart on the DAG structure
    46‚Üí	maxSearchLen := len(data)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí	for i := 0; i < maxSearchLen-33; i++ {
    49‚Üí		// Check for Protocol Buffer field markers that indicate a blob reference
    50‚Üí		if (data[i] == pbFieldTag1 || data[i] == pbFieldTag2) && i+1 < len(data) {
    51‚Üí			// Next byte should be the length of the blob ID
    52‚Üí			if data[i+1] == pbBlobIDLength && i+34 <= len(data) {
    53‚Üí				// Extract the 32-byte blob ID
    54‚Üí				idBytes := data[i+2 : i+34]
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí				// Validate that this looks like a blob ID by checking entropy.
    57‚Üí				// Real blob IDs are random bytes with high entropy, while false positives
    58‚Üí				// from message content tend to be ASCII text with low entropy.
    59‚Üí				isLikelyBlobID := false
    60‚Üí				nonPrintableCount := 0
    61‚Üí				for _, b := range idBytes {
    62‚Üí					if b < 0x20 || b > 0x7E {
    63‚Üí						nonPrintableCount++
    64‚Üí					}
    65‚Üí				}
    66‚Üí				// A real 32-byte blob ID will have ~75% non-printable bytes due to randomness.
    67‚Üí				// We use a lower threshold to avoid false negatives while still filtering out
    68‚Üí				// most ASCII text that might accidentally match the Protocol Buffer pattern.
    69‚Üí				if nonPrintableCount >= minNonPrintableBytes {
    70‚Üí					isLikelyBlobID = true
    71‚Üí				}
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí				if isLikelyBlobID {
    74‚Üí					// Convert to hex string
    75‚Üí					idHex := hex.EncodeToString(idBytes)
    76‚Üí					if !seenRefs[idHex] {
    77‚Üí						references = append(references, idHex)
    78‚Üí						seenRefs[idHex] = true
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí						// Uncomment this to see the reference counting in action when debugging the blob parser
    81‚Üí						// Safely truncate IDs for logging
    82‚Üí						referencedIDDisplay := idHex
    83‚Üí						if len(referencedIDDisplay) > 16 {
    84‚Üí							referencedIDDisplay = referencedIDDisplay[:16] + "..."
    85‚Üí						}
    86‚Üí						currentIDDisplay := currentBlobID
    87‚Üí						if len(currentIDDisplay) > 16 {
    88‚Üí							currentIDDisplay = currentIDDisplay[:16] + "..."
    89‚Üí						}
    90‚Üí						slog.Debug("Found blob reference",
    91‚Üí							"in_blob", currentIDDisplay,
    92‚Üí							"rowid", currentRowID,
    93‚Üí							"references_blob", referencedIDDisplay,
    94‚Üí							"at_byte_offset", i)
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí						i += 33 // Skip past this reference
    97‚Üí					}
    98‚Üí				}
    99‚Üí			}
   100‚Üí		}
   101‚Üí	}
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí	return references
   104‚Üí}
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí// extractJSONFromBinary attempts to extract JSON data from binary blob data.
   107‚Üí// Cursor stores JSON embedded in binary format. This function uses a proper JSON
   108‚Üí// decoder to extract valid JSON objects, which is more robust than manual parsing.
   109‚Üífunc extractJSONFromBinary(data []byte) []byte {
   110‚Üí	// Convert to string for easier pattern searching
   111‚Üí	dataStr := string(data)
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí	// Look for JSON object patterns that indicate the start of JSON data
   114‚Üí	jsonStart := -1
   115‚Üí	jsonPatterns := []string{`{"id":`, `{"role":`, `{"type":`, `{"content":`, `{"`}
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí	for _, pattern := range jsonPatterns {
   118‚Üí		if idx := strings.Index(dataStr, pattern); idx != -1 && (jsonStart == -1 || idx < jsonStart) {
   119‚Üí			jsonStart = idx
   120‚Üí		}
   121‚Üí	}
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí	if jsonStart == -1 {
   124‚Üí		return nil // No JSON found
   125‚Üí	}
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí	// Use json.Decoder to properly extract the JSON object
   128‚Üí	// This handles escaped characters, nested objects, and other edge cases correctly
   129‚Üí	decoder := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(data[jsonStart:]))
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí	// Use json.RawMessage to capture the JSON without parsing its structure
   132‚Üí	var result json.RawMessage
   133‚Üí	if err := decoder.Decode(&result); err != nil {
   134‚Üí		// If decoding fails, log and return nil
   135‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to decode JSON from binary data",
   136‚Üí			"error", err,
   137‚Üí			"dataOffset", jsonStart,
   138‚Üí			"dataPreview", string(data[jsonStart:min(jsonStart+50, len(data))]))
   139‚Üí		return nil
   140‚Üí	}
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí	return result
   143‚Üí}
   144‚Üí
```

```text
     1‚Üípackage cursorcli
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"database/sql"
     5‚Üí	"encoding/hex"
     6‚Üí	"encoding/json"
     7‚Üí	"fmt"
     8‚Üí	"log/slog"
     9‚Üí	"path/filepath"
    10‚Üí	"sort"
    11‚Üí	"strings"
    12‚Üí	"time"
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    15‚Üí	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // Pure Go SQLite driver
    16‚Üí)
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí// BlobRecord represents a raw blob from the Cursor SQLite database
    19‚Üítype BlobRecord struct {
    20‚Üí	RowID int             `json:"rowid"`
    21‚Üí	ID    string          `json:"id"`
    22‚Üí	Data  json.RawMessage `json:"data"` // Keep as raw JSON to preserve structure
    23‚Üí}
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí// extractFirstUserMessage extracts the full text of the first suitable user message
    26‚Üí// Returns empty string if no suitable message is found
    27‚Üífunc extractFirstUserMessage(blobRecords []BlobRecord) string {
    28‚Üí	for _, record := range blobRecords {
    29‚Üí		// Try to parse the data to check role and content
    30‚Üí		var data struct {
    31‚Üí			Role    string `json:"role"`
    32‚Üí			Content []struct {
    33‚Üí				Type string `json:"type"`
    34‚Üí				Text string `json:"text"`
    35‚Üí			} `json:"content"`
    36‚Üí		}
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí		if err := json.Unmarshal(record.Data, &data); err != nil {
    39‚Üí			continue // Skip if can't parse
    40‚Üí		}
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí		// Check if this is a user message
    43‚Üí		if data.Role != "user" {
    44‚Üí			continue
    45‚Üí		}
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí		// Check if we have content with text
    48‚Üí		if len(data.Content) == 0 || data.Content[0].Type != "text" {
    49‚Üí			continue
    50‚Üí		}
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí		text := data.Content[0].Text
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí		// Strip <user_query> tags if present
    55‚Üí		text = stripUserQueryTags(text)
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí		// Skip if starts with <user_info> (may be JSON-encoded as \u003cuser_info\u003e)
    58‚Üí		if strings.HasPrefix(text, "<user_info>") || strings.HasPrefix(text, "\u003cuser_info\u003e") {
    59‚Üí			continue
    60‚Üí		}
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí		// Return the full text
    63‚Üí		if strings.TrimSpace(text) != "" {
    64‚Üí			return text
    65‚Üí		}
    66‚Üí	}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí	return ""
    69‚Üí}
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí// extractSlugFromBlobs extracts a slug from the first suitable user message
    72‚Üífunc extractSlugFromBlobs(blobRecords []BlobRecord) string {
    73‚Üí	text := extractFirstUserMessage(blobRecords)
    74‚Üí	if text == "" {
    75‚Üí		slog.Debug("No suitable user message found for slug extraction")
    76‚Üí		return ""
    77‚Üí	}
    78‚Üí	slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(text)
    79‚Üí	slog.Debug("Extracted slug from user message", "slug", slug)
    80‚Üí	return slug
    81‚Üí}
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí// validateCursorDatabase validates that the SQLite database has the expected Cursor schema.
    84‚Üí// This ensures we're working with a genuine Cursor database and not some other SQLite file.
    85‚Üífunc validateCursorDatabase(db *sql.DB) error {
    86‚Üí	// Define the expected schema for a Cursor database
    87‚Üí	type tableSchema struct {
    88‚Üí		name    string
    89‚Üí		columns []string // Required columns
    90‚Üí	}
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí	requiredTables := []tableSchema{
    93‚Üí		{
    94‚Üí			name:    "blobs",
    95‚Üí			columns: []string{"id", "data"},
    96‚Üí		},
    97‚Üí		{
    98‚Üí			name:    "meta",
    99‚Üí			columns: []string{"key", "value"},
   100‚Üí		},
   101‚Üí	}
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí	// Validate each required table exists and has expected columns
   104‚Üí	for _, table := range requiredTables {
   105‚Üí		// Check if table exists
   106‚Üí		var tableExists int
   107‚Üí		err := db.QueryRow(
   108‚Üí			"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?",
   109‚Üí			table.name,
   110‚Üí		).Scan(&tableExists)
   111‚Üí		if err != nil {
   112‚Üí			return fmt.Errorf("failed to check for table %s: %w", table.name, err)
   113‚Üí		}
   114‚Üí		if tableExists == 0 {
   115‚Üí			return fmt.Errorf("invalid Cursor database: missing required table '%s'", table.name)
   116‚Üí		}
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí		// Validate columns exist in the table
   119‚Üí		// Note: pragma_table_info doesn't support parameter binding, so we use fmt.Sprintf
   120‚Üí		// This is safe because table.name comes from our hardcoded schema, not user input
   121‚Üí		for _, column := range table.columns {
   122‚Üí			var columnExists int
   123‚Üí			query := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pragma_table_info('%s') WHERE name=?", table.name)
   124‚Üí			err := db.QueryRow(query, column).Scan(&columnExists)
   125‚Üí			if err != nil {
   126‚Üí				return fmt.Errorf("failed to check column %s.%s: %w", table.name, column, err)
   127‚Üí			}
   128‚Üí			if columnExists == 0 {
   129‚Üí				return fmt.Errorf("invalid Cursor database: table '%s' missing required column '%s'", table.name, column)
   130‚Üí			}
   131‚Üí		}
   132‚Üí	}
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí	slog.Debug("Database schema validation passed")
   135‚Üí	return nil
   136‚Üí}
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí// getSessionTimestamp retrieves the session creation timestamp from the meta table
   139‚Üífunc getSessionTimestamp(db *sql.DB) (string, error) {
   140‚Üí	var rawValue string
   141‚Üí	err := db.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key='0'").Scan(&rawValue)
   142‚Üí	if err != nil {
   143‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to get metadata from meta table", "error", err)
   144‚Üí		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get metadata: %w", err)
   145‚Üí	}
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí	// The value is hex-encoded
   148‚Üí	jsonBytes, err := hex.DecodeString(rawValue)
   149‚Üí	if err != nil {
   150‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to decode hex metadata", "error", err)
   151‚Üí		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to decode hex: %w", err)
   152‚Üí	}
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí	// Parse the JSON
   155‚Üí	var metadata struct {
   156‚Üí		CreatedAt int64 `json:"createdAt"`
   157‚Üí	}
   158‚Üí	if err := json.Unmarshal(jsonBytes, &metadata); err != nil {
   159‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to parse metadata JSON", "error", err)
   160‚Üí		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse metadata: %w", err)
   161‚Üí	}
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí	slog.Debug("Found createdAt in metadata", "createdAt", metadata.CreatedAt)
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí	if metadata.CreatedAt > 0 {
   166‚Üí		// Convert milliseconds to time
   167‚Üí		ts := time.Unix(metadata.CreatedAt/1000, (metadata.CreatedAt%1000)*1000000)
   168‚Üí		slog.Debug("Converted timestamp", "timestamp", ts)
   169‚Üí		// Return as ISO 8601 format
   170‚Üí		return ts.Format(time.RFC3339), nil
   171‚Üí	}
   172‚Üí
   173‚Üí	// Return current time as fallback
   174‚Üí	return time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339), nil
   175‚Üí}
   176‚Üí
   177‚Üí// ReadSessionData reads all blobs from a Cursor session's store.db file
   178‚Üí// Returns the creation timestamp, slug, blob records in topologically sorted order, and orphaned blobs
   179‚Üífunc ReadSessionData(sessionPath string) (string, string, []BlobRecord, []BlobRecord, error) {
   180‚Üí	dbPath := filepath.Join(sessionPath, "store.db")
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí	slog.Debug("Opening Cursor CLI SQLite database", "path", dbPath)
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí	// Open the database in read-only mode
   185‚Üí	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath+"?mode=ro")
   186‚Üí	if err != nil {
   187‚Üí		return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open database: %w", err)
   188‚Üí	}
   189‚Üí	defer func() {
   190‚Üí		if err := db.Close(); err != nil {
   191‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to close database", "error", err)
   192‚Üí		}
   193‚Üí	}()
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí	slog.Debug("Successfully opened database", "path", dbPath)
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üí	// Enable WAL mode for non-blocking reads
   198‚Üí	// This prevents readers from blocking the cursor-agent writer
   199‚Üí	if _, err := db.Exec("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL"); err != nil {
   200‚Üí		// Log warning but continue - not fatal if WAL fails
   201‚Üí		slog.Warn("Failed to enable WAL mode", "error", err)
   202‚Üí	} else {
   203‚Üí		slog.Debug("Enabled WAL mode for non-blocking reads")
   204‚Üí	}
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí	// Validate that this is actually a Cursor database with the expected schema
   207‚Üí	if err := validateCursorDatabase(db); err != nil {
   208‚Üí		return "", "", nil, nil, err
   209‚Üí	}
   210‚Üí
   211‚Üí	// Get the session timestamp from metadata
   212‚Üí	createdAt, err := getSessionTimestamp(db)
   213‚Üí	if err != nil {
   214‚Üí		// Log but don't fail - use current time as fallback
   215‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to get session timestamp, using current time", "error", err)
   216‚Üí		createdAt = time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
   217‚Üí	}
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí	// Query all blobs
   220‚Üí	rows, err := db.Query("SELECT rowid, id, data FROM blobs")
   221‚Üí	if err != nil {
   222‚Üí		return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to query blobs: %w", err)
   223‚Üí	}
   224‚Üí	defer func() {
   225‚Üí		if err := rows.Close(); err != nil {
   226‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to close rows", "error", err)
   227‚Üí		}
   228‚Üí	}()
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üí	// Count total blobs for logging
   231‚Üí	var totalBlobs, jsonBlobs, skippedBlobs int
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí	slog.Debug("Starting to process blobs from database")
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí	// Collect all blobs and build a map for sorting
   236‚Üí	blobMap := make(map[string]*CursorBlob)
   237‚Üí	var allRowIDs []int // Collect all rowids for debugging
   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   240‚Üí		var rowid int
   241‚Üí		var id string
   242‚Üí		var data []byte
   243‚Üí
   244‚Üí		totalBlobs++
   245‚Üí
   246‚Üí		if err := rows.Scan(&rowid, &id, &data); err != nil {
   247‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to scan row", "error", err)
   248‚Üí			continue // Skip problematic rows
   249‚Üí		}
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí		// Collect rowid for debugging
   252‚Üí		allRowIDs = append(allRowIDs, rowid)
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí		// Log every single blob we process
   255‚Üí		idDisplay := id
   256‚Üí		if len(idDisplay) > 16 {
   257‚Üí			idDisplay = idDisplay[:16] + "..."
   258‚Üí		}
   259‚Üí		slog.Debug("Processing blob",
   260‚Üí			"rowid", rowid,
   261‚Üí			"id", idDisplay,
   262‚Üí			"data_len", len(data))
   263‚Üí
   264‚Üí		// Parse references from the binary data
   265‚Üí		references := parseReferences(data, id, rowid)
   266‚Üí
   267‚Üí		// Try to extract JSON from the binary data
   268‚Üí		jsonData := extractJSONFromBinary(data)
   269‚Üí		if jsonData != nil {
   270‚Üí			jsonBlobs++
   271‚Üí			blobMap[id] = &CursorBlob{
   272‚Üí				RowID:      rowid,
   273‚Üí				ID:         id,
   274‚Üí				Data:       json.RawMessage(jsonData),
   275‚Üí				RawData:    data,
   276‚Üí				References: references,
   277‚Üí			}
   278‚Üí		} else {
   279‚Üí			// If no JSON found, skip for now
   280‚Üí			// We may want to include these as reference-only blobs in the future
   281‚Üí			skippedBlobs++
   282‚Üí			// Uncomment this to see JSON detection in action when debugging the sqlite reader
   283‚Üí			idDisplay := id
   284‚Üí			if len(idDisplay) > 16 {
   285‚Üí				idDisplay = idDisplay[:16] + "..."
   286‚Üí			}
   287‚Üí			slog.Debug("Skipping blob (no JSON content found)",
   288‚Üí				"blob_id", idDisplay,
   289‚Üí				"rowid", rowid,
   290‚Üí				"has_references", len(references) > 0,
   291‚Üí				"reference_count", len(references))
   292‚Üí		}
   293‚Üí	}
   294‚Üí
   295‚Üí	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
   296‚Üí		return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("error iterating rows: %w", err)
   297‚Üí	}
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí	// Sort rowids for easier debugging
   300‚Üí	sort.Ints(allRowIDs)
   301‚Üí
   302‚Üí	// Log all rowids we got from the database - check if 1555-1560 are present
   303‚Üí	slog.Debug("All rowids from database query",
   304‚Üí		"count", len(allRowIDs),
   305‚Üí		"min", allRowIDs[0],
   306‚Üí		"max", allRowIDs[len(allRowIDs)-1],
   307‚Üí		"rowids", allRowIDs)
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí	slog.Debug("Blob processing summary",
   310‚Üí		"totalBlobs", totalBlobs,
   311‚Üí		"jsonBlobs", jsonBlobs,
   312‚Üí		"skippedBlobs", skippedBlobs)
   313‚Üí
   314‚Üí	// Perform topological sort on the blobs
   315‚Üí	sortedBlobs, orphanedBlobs, err := topologicalSort(blobMap)
   316‚Üí	if err != nil {
   317‚Üí		return "", "", nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to sort blobs: %w", err)
   318‚Üí	}
   319‚Üí
   320‚Üí	// Convert sorted blobs to BlobRecord array (without References and RawData)
   321‚Üí	var blobRecords []BlobRecord
   322‚Üí	for _, blob := range sortedBlobs {
   323‚Üí		blobRecords = append(blobRecords, BlobRecord{
   324‚Üí			RowID: blob.RowID,
   325‚Üí			ID:    blob.ID,
   326‚Üí			Data:  blob.Data,
   327‚Üí		})
   328‚Üí	}
   329‚Üí
   330‚Üí	// Convert orphaned blobs to BlobRecord array
   331‚Üí	var orphanRecords []BlobRecord
   332‚Üí	var orphanRowIDs []int
   333‚Üí	for _, blob := range orphanedBlobs {
   334‚Üí		orphanRecords = append(orphanRecords, BlobRecord{
   335‚Üí			RowID: blob.RowID,
   336‚Üí			ID:    blob.ID,
   337‚Üí			Data:  blob.Data,
   338‚Üí		})
   339‚Üí		orphanRowIDs = append(orphanRowIDs, blob.RowID)
   340‚Üí	}
   341‚Üí
   342‚Üí	if len(orphanRowIDs) > 0 {
   343‚Üí		sort.Ints(orphanRowIDs)
   344‚Üí		slog.Debug("Orphaned blob rowids", "rowids", orphanRowIDs, "count", len(orphanRowIDs))
   345‚Üí	}
   346‚Üí
   347‚Üí	// Log final DAG rowids before markdown generation
   348‚Üí	slog.Debug("Final DAG-sorted blob rowids before markdown generation:")
   349‚Üí	var rowids []int
   350‚Üí	for _, blob := range blobRecords {
   351‚Üí		rowids = append(rowids, blob.RowID)
   352‚Üí	}
   353‚Üí	slog.Debug("DAG rowids", "rowids", rowids, "count", len(rowids))
   354‚Üí
   355‚Üí	// Extract slug from the final blob records
   356‚Üí	slug := extractSlugFromBlobs(blobRecords)
   357‚Üí
   358‚Üí	return createdAt, slug, blobRecords, orphanRecords, nil
   359‚Üí}
   360‚Üí
   361‚Üí// min returns the minimum of two integers
   362‚Üífunc min(a, b int) int {
   363‚Üí	if a < b {
   364‚Üí		return a
   365‚Üí	}
   366‚Üí	return b
   367‚Üí}
   368‚Üí
```

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## Summary: Cursor CLI Provider Implementation - Agent Event to File Event Matching

### Overview

The Cursor CLI provider implementation in SpecStory tracks AI agent conversations and matches file system events to agent actions through a sophisticated provenance system. Here's the complete architecture:

---

## 1. Core Files and Their Responsibilities

### A. **agent_session.go** (613 lines)
**Purpose**: Converts Cursor's raw SQLite blob records into standardized SessionData objects

**Key Functions**:
- `GenerateAgentSession()`: Main entry point that converts blob records (already DAG-sorted) into exchanges
- `buildExchangesFromBlobs()`: Groups blobs into exchanges (user prompt + agent response cycles)
- `buildUserMessageFromBlob()`, `buildAgentMessagesFromBlob()`: Parse individual messages with tool calls
- `extractCursorPathHints()`: Extracts file paths from tool inputs (Write, StrReplace, Shell, Glob, etc.)
- `formatCursorToolWithSummary()`: Generates human-readable markdown summaries for each tool use

**Critical Logic**:
```
Blob Role Flow:
1. "user" blob ‚Üí Creates new exchange, starts a conversation
2. "assistant" blob ‚Üí Adds agent messages (may contain tool calls)
3. "tool" blob ‚Üí Merges tool results back into corresponding tool use

Tool Classification:
- Write/StrReplace/MultiStrReplace/Delete ‚Üí "write"
- Read ‚Üí "read"  
- Grep/Glob ‚Üí "search"
- Shell/LS ‚Üí "shell"
- TodoWrite ‚Üí "task"
```

**Path Extraction**:
- Looks for `path` and `file_path` fields in tool input
- Handles `MultiStrReplace` which has `paths[]` array
- Extracts from Shell commands using regex patterns
- Normalizes paths relative to workspace root

### B. **watcher.go** (357 lines)
**Purpose**: Monitors Cursor's SQLite database for changes in real-time

**Key Workflow**:
1. Polls every 5 seconds for new records in session databases
2. Tracks "known sessions" (existed at startup) vs "new sessions"
3. For new sessions, immediately processes and calls callback
4. For resumed sessions, checks for changes and processes incrementally
5. Uses message timestamp cache to preserve timestamps across regenerations

**Session State Tracking**:
- `lastCounts`: Maps sessionID to last-seen record count
- `knownSessions`: Sessions that existed at startup (skipped unless resumed)
- `resumedSession`: Session being actively worked on (monitored for changes)
- `tsCache`: MessageTimestampCache for consistent wall-clock timestamps

### C. **sqlite_reader.go** (368 lines)
**Purpose**: Low-level SQLite database access and blob extraction

**Key Functions**:
- `ReadSessionData()`: Opens store.db, reads all blobs, returns DAG-sorted records
- `validateCursorDatabase()`: Validates schema (requires `blobs` and `meta` tables)
- `getSessionTimestamp()`: Reads hex-encoded JSON metadata to extract createdAt
- `extractFirstUserMessage()`: Gets first real user message for slug generation
- `topologicalSort()`: Performs DAG sort on blob references (backward traversal from leaf)

**Database Details**:
- Enables WAL mode for non-blocking reads (cursor-agent can write while reading)
- Each blob has: rowid, id (32-byte hex), data (binary)
- Metadata stored in `meta` table with key='0' containing hex-encoded JSON
- Blobs that don't decode to JSON are skipped

### D. **blob_parser.go** (144 lines)
**Purpose**: Parses Protocol Buffer encoded blob references and extracts JSON

**Key Functions**:
- `parseReferences()`: Extracts blob ID references from binary data
  - Scans for Protocol Buffer field tags (0x0a, 0x12)
  - Validates 32-byte blob IDs by entropy check (‚â•8 non-printable bytes)
  - Returns list of referenced blob IDs
- `extractJSONFromBinary()`: Recovers JSON from binary blob data
  - Finds JSON patterns (`{"id":`, `{"role":`, etc.)
  - Uses `json.Decoder` for robust parsing

**Blob Reference Structure** (Protocol Buffer):
```
0x0a (field 1) or 0x12 (field 2) ‚Üí length (0x20 = 32) ‚Üí 32-byte blob ID
```

---

## 2. Debug Log Analysis (from 2026-02-20 16:00:29.890)

### Session Processing Flow

**Session ID**: `3a8084ec-289e-47d0-93a5-0ffd7082b51b`

**Key Events**:

1. **Initial Processing** (16:00:29.911):
   - Detected existing Cursor session
   - Found 34 total blobs in database
   - 14 blobs had JSON content, 20 skipped
   - Backward DAG traversal from leaf blob (rowid=35) found 10 connected, 4 orphaned

2. **Session Generation** (16:00:29.912):
   - DAG-sorted rowids: `[4 2 5 10 11 17 23 27 28 35]`
   - Extracted slug: `"changedir-into-foo-bar"`
   - Built 2 exchanges with multiple messages
   - **Failed to parse**: rowids 4 and 2 (content was string, not array)
   - Final: 2 exchanges generated successfully

3. **Critical Failure Point** (16:00:29.916):
   ```
   WARN: Failed to push agent event
   eventID=34dfd5762a73e2c9df3246d0db2d7bf4e7a1cbc95b22090e55805b4e184296af
   filePath=3.txt
   error="invalid agent event: timestamp is required"
   ```
   - Agent event was created but had missing timestamp field
   - Provenance engine rejected it

4. **New Session Detection** (16:01:04.922):
   - Watcher detected new session (4 records)
   - Generated session but NO exchanges (failed to parse main blob)
   - Wrote empty markdown file
   - Cloud sync initiated

5. **Session Update** (16:01:09.920):
   - Record count increased 4 ‚Üí 14
   - Re-processed session with same DAG-sorted structure
   - DAG rowids: `[4 2 5 9]`
   - Generated 1 exchange
   - **Again failed to parse**: rowids 4 and 2
   - Slug: `"create-a-hello-world"`
   - Updated markdown successfully

6. **File Event Matching Failure** (16:01:10.133):
   ```
   DEBUG: No agent match for file event
   fileEventID=1339ad1a-6629-467f-8b54-cd46123ec9b1
   filePath=/Users/sean/source/SpecStory/compositions/shell-3/hello.rb
   candidates=0
   ```
   - File watcher detected `hello.rb` creation
   - Attempted to match against agent events
   - **Result**: 0 candidates found
   - **Reason**: Agent event had no timestamp, wasn't pushed to provenance database

### Root Cause: Timestamp Generation Issue

The debug log reveals the core problem:

1. **Agent Session Generation** (in watcher context):
   - `tsCache` (MessageTimestampCache) IS passed to `GenerateAgentSession()`
   - Should assign timestamps via `assignTimestamps()`
   - But in sync mode (initial processing), `tsCache=nil`
   - This causes empty timestamps in messages

2. **Timestamp Assignment Logic** (`agent_session.go:117-138`):
   ```go
   func assignTimestamps(sd *SessionData, tsCache *MessageTimestampCache) {
       if tsCache == nil {
           return  // Timestamps left empty in sync mode
       }
       // Assign wall-clock times only in watch mode
   }
   ```

3. **Provenance Validation**:
   - Agent events require non-empty timestamp
   - Empty timestamp fails validation: `"timestamp is required"`
   - Event isn't pushed to provenance database
   - File event matching has no events to match against

---

## 3. Data Flow Diagram

  ‚îú‚îÄ parseReferences() [blob_parser.go] ‚Üí build blob DAG
  ‚îî‚îÄ topologicalSort() ‚Üí DAG-sorted blobs + orphans
       ‚Üì
GenerateAgentSession() [agent_session.go]
  ‚îú‚îÄ buildExchangesFromBlobs()
  ‚îÇ  ‚îú‚îÄ buildUserMessageFromBlob()

## 5. Key Implementation Details

### A. Message ID Format
- User messages: `row-{rowid}`
- Agent text messages: `row-{rowid}`
- Agent tool messages: `row-{rowid}-tool-{index}`
- Example: `row-16-tool-0` = rowid 16, first tool call

### B. Exchange Grouping Logic
```
- User message ‚Üí starts new exchange
- Assistant messages ‚Üí added to current exchange
- Tool messages ‚Üí merged into corresponding tool use
- Each user message starts fresh exchange
```

### C. Path Hints Extraction Priority
1. Direct `path` field (Write, Delete, Read)
2. `file_path` field (StrReplace, MultiStrReplace)
3. `paths[]` array items (MultiStrReplace)
4. Shell command parsing (command field in Shell tool)

### D. Tool Type Classification
Maps Cursor tool names to standard types for unified handling across providers

### E. Orphaned Blob Handling
- Blobs unreachable from leaf node via backward traversal
- Identified but excluded from main DAG
- Logged separately in debug output
- Not included in SessionData

---

## 6. Provenance Database

**Location**: `/Users/sean/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db`
**Files**:
- `provenance.db` (main database)
- `provenance.db-shm` (SQLite shared memory)
- `provenance.db-wal` (Write-Ahead Log)

**Purpose**: Stores agent events extracted from SessionData for matching with file system events

**Match Window**: 5 seconds (configured in debug log)

---

## 7. Markdown Generation

Output location: `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/compositions/shell-3/.specstory/history/2026-02-20_21-00-31Z-create-a-hello-world.md`

**Structure**:
- Exchanges rendered as conversation flow
- Each message with timestamp
- Tool uses formatted with summaries and command outputs
- Thinking/reasoning in `<think>` tags
- Code blocks preserved with language hints

**Tool Formatting** (from `agent_session.go:508-590`):
- **Write**: Shows file path in summary + code block
- **StrReplace**: Diff-style formatting
- **Delete**: File path in summary
- **Grep/Glob**: Pattern in summary
- **Shell**: Command in summary, output in code block
- **TodoWrite**: Custom todo list formatting

---

## Summary

The Cursor CLI provider's agent-to-file matching works through:

1. **Blob Parsing**: Protocol Buffer format with entropy-validated blob IDs
2. **DAG Sorting**: Backward traversal from leaf node identifies conversation flow
3. **Session Generation**: Converts blobs to standardized SessionData with timestamps
4. **Path Extraction**: Pulls file paths from tool inputs for provenance tracking
5. **Event Matching**: Timestamp-based matching in 5-second window

**Current Issue**: Initial session processing uses `tsCache=nil` in sync mode, causing empty timestamps, which breaks provenance event validation. Watch mode correctly uses `tsCache` for wall-clock timestamps.

    15‚Üí// Type aliases for convenience - use the shared schema types

    17‚Üí	SessionData  = schema.SessionData
    18‚Üí	ProviderInfo = schema.ProviderInfo

    25‚Üí// MessageTimestampCache tracks first-seen wall clock times for Cursor messages.
    26‚Üí// Cursor's SQLite data doesn't include per-message timestamps, so in run/watch
    27‚Üí// mode we stamp each message with time.Now() when we first see it. The cache

    30‚Üítype MessageTimestampCache struct {

    33‚Üí}
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üífunc NewMessageTimestampCache() *MessageTimestampCache {
    36‚Üí	return &MessageTimestampCache{times: make(map[string]time.Time)}
    37‚Üí}
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí// Stamp returns the first-seen timestamp for a message ID.
    40‚Üí// First call for a given ID records time.Now(); subsequent calls return the cached value.
    41‚Üífunc (c *MessageTimestampCache) Stamp(messageID string) time.Time {
    42‚Üí	c.mu.Lock()
    43‚Üí	defer c.mu.Unlock()
    44‚Üí	if t, ok := c.times[messageID]; ok {
    45‚Üí		return t
    46‚Üí	}
    47‚Üí	now := time.Now()
    48‚Üí	c.times[messageID] = now
    49‚Üí	return now
    50‚Üí}
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí// GenerateAgentSession creates a SessionData from Cursor CLI blob records.
    53‚Üí// The blob records should already be DAG-sorted from ReadSessionData.
    54‚Üí// When tsCache is non-nil (run/watch mode), timestamps are assigned to messages
    55‚Üí// using wall clock time. When nil (sync mode), timestamps are left empty.
    56‚Üífunc GenerateAgentSession(blobRecords []BlobRecord, workspaceRoot, sessionID, createdAt, slug string, tsCache *MessageTimestampCache) (*SessionData, error) {
    57‚Üí	slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Starting", "sessionID", sessionID, "blobCount", len(blobRecords))
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí	if len(blobRecords) == 0 {
    60‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no blob records to process")
    61‚Üí	}
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí	// Group records into exchanges
    64‚Üí	exchanges, err := buildExchangesFromBlobs(blobRecords, workspaceRoot)
    65‚Üí	if err != nil {
    66‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to build exchanges: %w", err)
    67‚Üí	}
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí	// Assign exchangeId to each exchange (format: sessionId:index)
    70‚Üí	for i := range exchanges {
    71‚Üí		exchanges[i].ExchangeID = fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", sessionID, i)
    72‚Üí	}
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí	slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Built exchanges", "count", len(exchanges))
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí	// Populate Summary and FormattedMarkdown for all tools using existing Cursor formatters
    77‚Üí	for i := range exchanges {
    78‚Üí		for j := range exchanges[i].Messages {
    79‚Üí			msg := &exchanges[i].Messages[j]
    80‚Üí			if msg.Tool != nil {
    81‚Üí				summary, formattedMd := formatCursorToolWithSummary(msg.Tool)
    82‚Üí				if summary != "" {
    83‚Üí					msg.Tool.Summary = &summary
    84‚Üí				}
    85‚Üí				if formattedMd != "" {
    86‚Üí					msg.Tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formattedMd
    87‚Üí				}
    88‚Üí			}
    89‚Üí		}
    90‚Üí	}
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí	sessionData := &SessionData{
    93‚Üí		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
    94‚Üí		Provider: ProviderInfo{
    95‚Üí			ID:      "cursor",
    96‚Üí			Name:    "Cursor CLI",
    97‚Üí			Version: "unknown", // Cursor doesn't expose version in SQLite
    98‚Üí		},
    99‚Üí		SessionID:     sessionID,
   100‚Üí		CreatedAt:     createdAt,
   101‚Üí		Slug:          slug,
   102‚Üí		WorkspaceRoot: workspaceRoot,
   103‚Üí		Exchanges:     exchanges,
   104‚Üí	}
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí	assignTimestamps(sessionData, tsCache)
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí	return sessionData, nil
   109‚Üí}
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí// assignTimestamps fills in Message.Timestamp and Exchange start/end times for
   112‚Üí// run/watch mode only. Cursor's data doesn't include per-message timestamps,
   113‚Üí// so we use wall clock time when each message is first seen. The cache ensures
   114‚Üí// previously-seen messages retain their original timestamp across session
   115‚Üí// regenerations. When tsCache is nil (sync mode), timestamps are left empty
   116‚Üí// so they don't render in the markdown output.
   117‚Üífunc assignTimestamps(sd *SessionData, tsCache *MessageTimestampCache) {
   118‚Üí	if tsCache == nil {
   119‚Üí		return
   120‚Üí	}
   121‚Üí	for i := range sd.Exchanges {
   122‚Üí		ex := &sd.Exchanges[i]
   123‚Üí		for j := range ex.Messages {
   124‚Üí			msg := &ex.Messages[j]
   125‚Üí			if msg.Timestamp == "" {
   126‚Üí				msg.Timestamp = tsCache.Stamp(msg.ID).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   127‚Üí			}
   128‚Üí		}
   129‚Üí		if len(ex.Messages) > 0 {
   130‚Üí			if ex.StartTime == "" {
   131‚Üí				ex.StartTime = ex.Messages[0].Timestamp
   132‚Üí			}
   133‚Üí			if ex.EndTime == "" {
   134‚Üí				ex.EndTime = ex.Messages[len(ex.Messages)-1].Timestamp
   135‚Üí			}
   136‚Üí		}
   137‚Üí	}
   138‚Üí}
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üí// buildExchangesFromBlobs groups blob records into exchanges
   141‚Üí// An exchange is a user prompt followed by agent responses and tool results
   142‚Üí// Blob records are already in DAG-sorted order from ReadSessionData
   143‚Üífunc buildExchangesFromBlobs(blobRecords []BlobRecord, workspaceRoot string) ([]Exchange, error) {
   144‚Üí	var exchanges []Exchange
   145‚Üí	var currentExchange *Exchange
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí	// Track pending tool calls that need results merged
   148‚Üí	type pendingTool struct {
   149‚Üí		exchangeIdx int
   150‚Üí		messageIdx  int
   151‚Üí		toolCallID  string
   152‚Üí	}
   153‚Üí	pendingTools := make(map[string]pendingTool)
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí	for _, record := range blobRecords {
   156‚Üí		// Parse the blob data
   157‚Üí		var data struct {
   158‚Üí			Role    string            `json:"role"`
   159‚Üí			ID      string            `json:"id"`
   160‚Üí			Content []json.RawMessage `json:"content"`
   161‚Üí		}
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí		if err := json.Unmarshal(record.Data, &data); err != nil {
   164‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to parse blob record", "rowid", record.RowID, "error", err)
   165‚Üí			continue
   166‚Üí		}
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí		// Skip if no content
   169‚Üí		if len(data.Content) == 0 {
   170‚Üí			continue
   171‚Üí		}
   172‚Üí
   173‚Üí		switch data.Role {
   174‚Üí		case "user":
   175‚Üí			// Check if this is a real user message or just metadata
   176‚Üí			firstContent, skipMsg := parseFirstContent(data.Content[0])
   177‚Üí			if skipMsg || (firstContent.Type == "text" && strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(firstContent.Text), "<user_info>")) {
   178‚Üí				continue
   179‚Üí			}
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí			// This is a new user prompt, start a new exchange
   182‚Üí			if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
   183‚Üí				exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
   184‚Üí			}
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üí			currentExchange = &Exchange{
   187‚Üí				Messages: []Message{},
   188‚Üí			}
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí			msg := buildUserMessageFromBlob(data.Content, record.RowID)
   191‚Üí			currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, msg)
   192‚Üí			if msg.Timestamp != "" {
   193‚Üí				currentExchange.StartTime = msg.Timestamp
   194‚Üí			}
   195‚Üí
   196‚Üí		case "assistant":
   197‚Üí			// Agent message - add to current exchange
   198‚Üí			if currentExchange == nil {
   199‚Üí				// Create exchange if we don't have one (shouldn't happen normally)
   200‚Üí				currentExchange = &Exchange{
   201‚Üí					Messages: []Message{},
   202‚Üí				}
   203‚Üí			}
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí			// Build messages from this assistant blob (may return multiple if there are multiple tool calls)
   206‚Üí			msgs := buildAgentMessagesFromBlob(data.Content, record.RowID, workspaceRoot)
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí			// Add all messages and track tool calls
   209‚Üí			for _, msg := range msgs {
   210‚Üí				// Track tool calls for later result merging
   211‚Üí				if msg.Tool != nil && msg.Tool.UseID != "" {
   212‚Üí					pendingTools[msg.Tool.UseID] = pendingTool{
   213‚Üí						exchangeIdx: len(exchanges),
   214‚Üí						messageIdx:  len(currentExchange.Messages),
   215‚Üí						toolCallID:  msg.Tool.UseID,
   216‚Üí					}
   217‚Üí				}
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí				currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, msg)
   220‚Üí				if msg.Timestamp != "" {
   221‚Üí					currentExchange.EndTime = msg.Timestamp
   222‚Üí				}
   223‚Üí			}
   224‚Üí
   225‚Üí		case "tool":
   226‚Üí			// Tool result - merge into the corresponding tool use
   227‚Üí			if currentExchange == nil {
   228‚Üí				continue
   229‚Üí			}
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí			// Extract tool result content
   232‚Üí			for _, contentRaw := range data.Content {
   233‚Üí				var contentItem struct {
   234‚Üí					Type       string `json:"type"`
   235‚Üí					ToolCallID string `json:"toolCallId"`
   236‚Üí					ToolName   string `json:"toolName"`
   237‚Üí					Result     string `json:"result"`
   238‚Üí				}
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí				if err := json.Unmarshal(contentRaw, &contentItem); err != nil {
   241‚Üí					continue
   242‚Üí				}
   243‚Üí
   244‚Üí				if contentItem.Type == "tool-result" && contentItem.ToolCallID != "" {
   245‚Üí					// Find the pending tool and merge the result
   246‚Üí					if pending, ok := pendingTools[contentItem.ToolCallID]; ok {
   247‚Üí						// Merge result into the tool message
   248‚Üí						mergeToolResultIntoMessage(currentExchange, pending.messageIdx, contentItem.Result, contentItem.ToolName)
   249‚Üí						delete(pendingTools, contentItem.ToolCallID)
   250‚Üí					}
   251‚Üí				}
   252‚Üí			}
   253‚Üí		}
   254‚Üí	}
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí	// Add the last exchange if it exists
   257‚Üí	if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
   258‚Üí		exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
   259‚Üí	}
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí	// Blob records are already in DAG-sorted order, so exchanges are too
   262‚Üí	return exchanges, nil
   263‚Üí}
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí// parseFirstContent parses the first content item to check if message should be skipped
   266‚Üífunc parseFirstContent(contentRaw json.RawMessage) (ContentPart, bool) {
   267‚Üí	var contentItem struct {
   268‚Üí		Type string `json:"type"`
   269‚Üí		Text string `json:"text"`
   270‚Üí	}
   271‚Üí
   272‚Üí	if err := json.Unmarshal(contentRaw, &contentItem); err != nil {
   273‚Üí		return ContentPart{}, false
   274‚Üí	}
   275‚Üí
   276‚Üí	// Skip if it's user_info
   277‚Üí	if contentItem.Type == "text" && strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(contentItem.Text), "<user_info>") {
   278‚Üí		return ContentPart{Type: "text", Text: contentItem.Text}, true
   279‚Üí	}
   280‚Üí
   281‚Üí	return ContentPart{Type: contentItem.Type, Text: contentItem.Text}, false
   282‚Üí}
   283‚Üí
   284‚Üí// buildUserMessageFromBlob creates a Message from a user blob
   285‚Üífunc buildUserMessageFromBlob(content []json.RawMessage, rowID int) Message {
   286‚Üí	var contentParts []ContentPart
   287‚Üí
   288‚Üí	for _, contentRaw := range content {
   289‚Üí		var contentItem struct {
   290‚Üí			Type string `json:"type"`
   291‚Üí			Text string `json:"text"`
   292‚Üí		}
   293‚Üí
   294‚Üí		if err := json.Unmarshal(contentRaw, &contentItem); err != nil {
   295‚Üí			continue
   296‚Üí		}
   297‚Üí
   298‚Üí		if contentItem.Type == "text" && contentItem.Text != "" {
   299‚Üí			// Strip user_query tags if present
   300‚Üí			cleanedText := stripUserQueryTags(contentItem.Text)
   301‚Üí			if cleanedText != "" {
   302‚Üí				contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
   303‚Üí					Type: "text",
   304‚Üí					Text: cleanedText,
   305‚Üí				})
   306‚Üí			}
   307‚Üí		}
   308‚Üí	}
   309‚Üí
   310‚Üí	return Message{
   311‚Üí		ID:      fmt.Sprintf("row-%d", rowID),
   312‚Üí		Role:    "user",
   313‚Üí		Content: contentParts,
   314‚Üí	}
   315‚Üí}
   316‚Üí
   317‚Üí// buildAgentMessagesFromBlob creates Messages from an assistant blob
   318‚Üí// When there are multiple tool-calls in a single assistant message, this creates
   319‚Üí// one text message followed by separate messages for each tool call
   320‚Üífunc buildAgentMessagesFromBlob(content []json.RawMessage, rowID int, workspaceRoot string) []Message {
   321‚Üí	var messages []Message
   322‚Üí	var contentParts []ContentPart
   323‚Üí	toolCallIndex := 0
   324‚Üí
   325‚Üí	for _, contentRaw := range content {
   326‚Üí		var contentItem struct {
   327‚Üí			Type       string          `json:"type"`
   328‚Üí			Text       string          `json:"text"`
   329‚Üí			ToolName   string          `json:"toolName"`
   330‚Üí			ToolCallID string          `json:"toolCallId"`
   331‚Üí			Args       json.RawMessage `json:"args"`
   332‚Üí		}
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üí		if err := json.Unmarshal(contentRaw, &contentItem); err != nil {
   335‚Üí			continue
   336‚Üí		}
   337‚Üí
   338‚Üí		switch contentItem.Type {
   339‚Üí		case "text":
   340‚Üí			if contentItem.Text != "" {
   341‚Üí				contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
   342‚Üí					Type: "text",
   343‚Üí					Text: contentItem.Text,
   344‚Üí				})
   345‚Üí			}
   346‚Üí
   347‚Üí		case "reasoning":
   348‚Üí			// Preserve reasoning content as thinking type (schema canonical name)
   349‚Üí			if contentItem.Text != "" {
   350‚Üí				contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
   351‚Üí					Type: "thinking",
   352‚Üí					Text: contentItem.Text,
   353‚Üí				})
   354‚Üí			}
   355‚Üí
   356‚Üí		case "tool-call":
   357‚Üí			// Parse tool arguments
   358‚Üí			var toolInput map[string]interface{}
   359‚Üí			if err := json.Unmarshal(contentItem.Args, &toolInput); err != nil {
   360‚Üí				continue
   361‚Üí			}
   362‚Üí
   363‚Üí			// Create a separate message for this tool call
   364‚Üí			toolMsg := Message{
   365‚Üí				ID:   fmt.Sprintf("row-%d-tool-%d", rowID, toolCallIndex),
   366‚Üí				Role: "agent",
   367‚Üí				Tool: &ToolInfo{
   368‚Üí					Name:  contentItem.ToolName,
   369‚Üí					Type:  classifyCursorToolType(contentItem.ToolName),
   370‚Üí					UseID: contentItem.ToolCallID,
   371‚Üí					Input: toolInput,
   372‚Üí				},
   373‚Üí				PathHints: extractCursorPathHints(toolInput, workspaceRoot),
   374‚Üí			}
   375‚Üí
   376‚Üí			messages = append(messages, toolMsg)
   377‚Üí			toolCallIndex++
   378‚Üí		}
   379‚Üí	}
   380‚Üí
   381‚Üí	// If there's text content, create a text message first
   382‚Üí	if len(contentParts) > 0 {
   383‚Üí		textMsg := Message{
   384‚Üí			ID:      fmt.Sprintf("row-%d", rowID),
   385‚Üí			Role:    "agent",
   386‚Üí			Content: contentParts,
   387‚Üí		}
   388‚Üí		// Insert text message at the beginning
   389‚Üí		messages = append([]Message{textMsg}, messages...)
   390‚Üí	}
   391‚Üí
   392‚Üí	// If no messages were created (shouldn't happen), return an empty text message
   393‚Üí	if len(messages) == 0 {
   394‚Üí		messages = append(messages, Message{
   395‚Üí			ID:      fmt.Sprintf("row-%d", rowID),
   396‚Üí			Role:    "agent",
   397‚Üí			Content: []ContentPart{},
   398‚Üí		})
   399‚Üí	}
   400‚Üí
   401‚Üí	return messages
   402‚Üí}
   403‚Üí
   404‚Üí// mergeToolResultIntoMessage merges a tool result into a tool use message
   405‚Üífunc mergeToolResultIntoMessage(exchange *Exchange, messageIdx int, result, toolName string) {
   406‚Üí	if messageIdx >= len(exchange.Messages) {
   407‚Üí		return
   408‚Üí	}
   409‚Üí
   410‚Üí	msg := &exchange.Messages[messageIdx]
   411‚Üí	if msg.Tool == nil {
   412‚Üí		return
   413‚Üí	}
   414‚Üí
   415‚Üí	// Skip TodoWrite errors
   416‚Üí	if toolName == "TodoWrite" && strings.HasPrefix(result, "Invalid arguments:") {
   417‚Üí		return
   418‚Üí	}
   419‚Üí
   420‚Üí	// Clean TodoWrite results
   421‚Üí	if toolName == "TodoWrite" {
   422‚Üí		result = cleanTodoResult(result)
   423‚Üí	}
   424‚Üí
   425‚Üí	// Add the output to the tool
   426‚Üí	msg.Tool.Output = map[string]interface{}{
   427‚Üí		"content": result,
   428‚Üí	}
   429‚Üí}
   430‚Üí
   431‚Üí// classifyCursorToolType maps Cursor tool names to standard tool types
   432‚Üífunc classifyCursorToolType(toolName string) string {
   433‚Üí	switch toolName {
   434‚Üí	case "Write", "StrReplace", "MultiStrReplace":
   435‚Üí		return "write"
   436‚Üí	case "Read":
   437‚Üí		return "read"
   438‚Üí	case "Grep", "Glob":
   439‚Üí		return "search"
   440‚Üí	case "Shell", "LS":
   441‚Üí		return "shell"
   442‚Üí	case "Delete":
   443‚Üí		return "write" // Delete is a write operation
   444‚Üí	case "TodoWrite":
   445‚Üí		return "task"
   446‚Üí	default:
   447‚Üí		return "unknown"
   448‚Üí	}
   449‚Üí}
   450‚Üí
   451‚Üí// extractCursorPathHints extracts file paths from Cursor tool input
   452‚Üífunc extractCursorPathHints(input map[string]interface{}, workspaceRoot string) []string {
   453‚Üí	var paths []string
   454‚Üí
   455‚Üí	// Common path field names in Cursor tools
   456‚Üí	pathFields := []string{"path", "file_path"}
   457‚Üí
   458‚Üí	for _, field := range pathFields {
   459‚Üí		if value, ok := input[field].(string); ok && value != "" {
   460‚Üí			normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
   461‚Üí			if !containsPath(paths, normalizedPath) {
   462‚Üí				paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
   463‚Üí			}
   464‚Üí		}
   465‚Üí	}
   466‚Üí
   467‚Üí	// Handle MultiStrReplace paths array
   468‚Üí	if pathsArray, ok := input["paths"].([]interface{}); ok {
   469‚Üí		for _, item := range pathsArray {
   470‚Üí			if pathObj, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
   471‚Üí				if filePath, ok := pathObj["file_path"].(string); ok && filePath != "" {
   472‚Üí					normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(filePath, workspaceRoot)
   473‚Üí					if !containsPath(paths, normalizedPath) {
   474‚Üí						paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
   475‚Üí					}
   476‚Üí				}
   477‚Üí			}
   478‚Üí		}
   479‚Üí	}
   480‚Üí
   481‚Üí	// Extract paths from shell commands (redirect targets, file-creating commands).
   482‚Üí	// Cursor's Shell tool input only contains "command" and "description" ‚Äî no CWD field.
   483‚Üí	// The tool result also lacks CWD info. So we fall back to workspaceRoot as the best
   484‚Üí	// approximation. This means relative paths in commands run after a `cd` will resolve
   485‚Üí	// against the wrong directory, but there's nothing better available from Cursor's data.
   486‚Üí	if command, ok := input["command"].(string); ok && command != "" {
   487‚Üí		shellPaths := spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, workspaceRoot, workspaceRoot)
   488‚Üí		for _, sp := range shellPaths {
   489‚Üí			if !containsPath(paths, sp) {
   490‚Üí				paths = append(paths, sp)
   491‚Üí			}
   492‚Üí		}
   493‚Üí	}
   494‚Üí
   495‚Üí	return paths
   496‚Üí}
   497‚Üí
   498‚Üí// containsPath checks if a string slice contains a path
   499‚Üífunc containsPath(slice []string, value string) bool {
   500‚Üí	for _, item := range slice {
   501‚Üí		if item == value {
   502‚Üí			return true
   503‚Üí		}
   504‚Üí	}
   505‚Üí	return false
   506‚Üí}
   507‚Üí
   508‚Üí// formatCursorToolWithSummary generates custom summary and formatted markdown for a Cursor tool
   509‚Üí// Returns (summary, formattedMarkdown) where summary is the custom content for <summary> tag
   510‚Üí// and formattedMarkdown is additional content to display in the tool-use block
   511‚Üífunc formatCursorToolWithSummary(tool *ToolInfo) (string, string) {
   512‚Üí	var summary string
   513‚Üí	var formattedMd strings.Builder
   514‚Üí
   515‚Üí	// Format tool use based on tool name using existing Cursor formatters
   516‚Üí	// These formatters return custom summary content (without "Tool use: **Name**" prefix)
   517‚Üí	if tool.Input != nil {
   518‚Üí		switch tool.Name {
   519‚Üí		case "Write":
   520‚Üí			// Write tool: summary has file path, formatted content has code block
   521‚Üí			if path, ok := tool.Input["path"].(string); ok && path != "" {
   522‚Üí				summary = fmt.Sprintf("`%s`", path)
   523‚Üí			}
   524‚Üí			formattedMd.WriteString(formatWriteTool(tool.Input))
   525‚Üí		case "StrReplace":
   526‚Üí			// StrReplace tool: summary is empty, formatted content has diff
   527‚Üí			formattedMd.WriteString(formatStrReplaceTool(tool.Input))
   528‚Üí		case "Delete":
   529‚Üí			// Delete tool: summary has path, no formatted content
   530‚Üí			summary = formatDeleteTool(tool.Input)
   531‚Üí		case "Grep":
   532‚Üí			// Grep tool: summary has search details, no formatted content
   533‚Üí			summary = formatGrepTool(tool.Input)
   534‚Üí		case "Glob":
   535‚Üí			// Glob tool: summary has pattern, no formatted content
   536‚Üí			summary = formatGlobTool(tool.Input)
   537‚Üí		case "Read":
   538‚Üí			// Read tool: summary has file path, no formatted content
   539‚Üí			summary = formatReadTool(tool.Input)
   540‚Üí		case "LS":
   541‚Üí			// LS tool: summary has directory, no formatted content
   542‚Üí			summary = formatLSTool(tool.Input)
   543‚Üí		case "Shell":
   544‚Üí			// Shell tool: summary has command, no formatted content
   545‚Üí			summary = formatShellTool(tool.Input)
   546‚Üí		case "MultiStrReplace":
   547‚Üí			// MultiStrReplace tool: summary has file and edits, no formatted content
   548‚Üí			summary = formatMultiStrReplaceTool(tool.Input)
   549‚Üí		case "TodoWrite":
   550‚Üí			// Handle TodoWrite specially
   551‚Üí			if todos, ok := tool.Input["todos"].([]interface{}); ok && len(todos) > 0 {
   552‚Üí				formattedMd.WriteString(formatTodoList(todos))
   553‚Üí			}
   554‚Üí		}
   555‚Üí	}
   556‚Üí
   557‚Üí	// Add tool result/output if present
   558‚Üí	if tool.Output != nil {
   559‚Üí		if content, ok := tool.Output["content"].(string); ok && content != "" {
   560‚Üí			if formattedMd.Len() > 0 {
   561‚Üí				formattedMd.WriteString("\n\n")
   562‚Üí			}
   563‚Üí			// Format based on tool type
   564‚Üí			switch tool.Name {
   565‚Üí			case "TodoWrite":
   566‚Üí				// TodoWrite results are already formatted
   567‚Üí				formattedMd.WriteString(content)
   568‚Üí			case "Grep":
   569‚Üí				// Grep results have special formatting
   570‚Üí				formattedMd.WriteString(formatGrepResult(content))
   571‚Üí			case "Glob":
   572‚Üí				// Glob results are inline
   573‚Üí				formattedMd.WriteString(strings.TrimSpace(content))
   574‚Üí			case "Delete":
   575‚Üí				// Delete results are inline
   576‚Üí				formattedMd.WriteString(content)
   577‚Üí			default:
   578‚Üí				// Default: wrap in code block
   579‚Üí				formattedMd.WriteString(formatToolResult(content))
   580‚Üí			}
   581‚Üí		}
   582‚Üí	}
   583‚Üí
   584‚Üí	// If we have a custom summary, prepend "Tool use: **ToolName**" prefix
   585‚Üí	if summary != "" {
   586‚Üí		summary = fmt.Sprintf("Tool use: **%s** %s", tool.Name, summary)
   587‚Üí	}
   588‚Üí
   589‚Üí	return summary, strings.TrimSpace(formattedMd.String())
   590‚Üí}
   591‚Üí
   592‚Üí// stripUserQueryTags removes <user_query> tags from text content
   593‚Üí// These tags are added by Cursor CLI to wrap user input, but we don't want them in the markdown output
   594‚Üífunc stripUserQueryTags(text string) string {
   595‚Üí	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   596‚Üí
   597‚Üí	// Check if text is wrapped with user_query tags
   598‚Üí	if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "<user_query>") && strings.HasSuffix(trimmed, "</user_query>") {
   599‚Üí		// Remove opening tag and any trailing newline
   600‚Üí		content := strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "<user_query>")
   601‚Üí		content = strings.TrimPrefix(content, "\n")
   602‚Üí
   603‚Üí		// Remove closing tag and any preceding newline
   604‚Üí		content = strings.TrimSuffix(content, "</user_query>")
   605‚Üí		content = strings.TrimSuffix(content, "\n")
   606‚Üí
   607‚Üí		return content
   608‚Üí	}
   609‚Üí
   610‚Üí	// Return original text if tags aren't present
   611‚Üí	return text
   612‚Üí}
   613‚Üí
```

    18‚Üí// CursorWatcher monitors Cursor SQLite databases for changes
    19‚Üítype CursorWatcher struct {
    20‚Üí	projectPath     string
    21‚Üí	hashDir         string // The hash directory for this project
    22‚Üí	pollInterval    time.Duration
    23‚Üí	ctx             context.Context
    24‚Üí	cancel          context.CancelFunc
    25‚Üí	wg              sync.WaitGroup
    26‚Üí	callbackWg      sync.WaitGroup              // Track callback goroutines
    27‚Üí	lastCounts      map[string]int              // Track record counts per session
    28‚Üí	knownSessions   map[string]bool             // Sessions that existed at startup (don't process unless resumed)
    29‚Üí	resumedSession  string                      // Session ID being resumed (watch for changes)
    30‚Üí	sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession) // Callback for session updates
    31‚Üí	debugRaw        bool                        // Whether to write debug raw data files
    32‚Üí	tsCache         *MessageTimestampCache      // Tracks first-seen timestamps for messages in run/watch mode
    33‚Üí	mu              sync.RWMutex                // Protects maps
    34‚Üí}
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí// NewCursorWatcher creates a new Cursor database watcher
    37‚Üífunc NewCursorWatcher(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) (*CursorWatcher, error) {
    38‚Üí	// Get the project hash directory
    39‚Üí	hashDir, err := GetProjectHashDir(projectPath)
    40‚Üí	if err != nil {
    41‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get project hash directory: %w", err)
    42‚Üí	}
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí	return &CursorWatcher{
    47‚Üí		projectPath:     projectPath,
    48‚Üí		hashDir:         hashDir,
    49‚Üí		pollInterval:    5 * time.Second, // 5-second polling interval
    50‚Üí		ctx:             ctx,
    51‚Üí		cancel:          cancel,
    52‚Üí		lastCounts:      make(map[string]int),
    53‚Üí		knownSessions:   make(map[string]bool),
    54‚Üí		resumedSession:  "",
    55‚Üí		sessionCallback: sessionCallback,
    56‚Üí		debugRaw:        debugRaw,
    57‚Üí		tsCache:         NewMessageTimestampCache(),
    58‚Üí	}, nil
    59‚Üí}
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí// SetInitialState sets the sessions that existed at startup and which session is being resumed
    62‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) SetInitialState(existingSessionIDs map[string]bool, resumedSessionID string) {
    63‚Üí	w.mu.Lock()
    64‚Üí	defer w.mu.Unlock()
    65‚Üí	w.knownSessions = existingSessionIDs
    66‚Üí	w.resumedSession = resumedSessionID
    67‚Üí	if resumedSessionID != "" {
    68‚Üí		slog.Info("Watcher will monitor resumed session for changes", "sessionId", resumedSessionID)
    69‚Üí	}
    70‚Üí	slog.Debug("Watcher initialized with existing sessions", "count", len(existingSessionIDs))
    71‚Üí}
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí// Start begins monitoring the Cursor databases
    74‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) Start() error {
    75‚Üí	slog.Info("Starting Cursor CLI watcher",
    76‚Üí		"projectPath", w.projectPath,
    77‚Üí		"hashDir", w.hashDir,
    78‚Üí		"pollInterval", w.pollInterval)
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí	// Check if hash directory exists
    81‚Üí	stat, err := os.Stat(w.hashDir)
    82‚Üí	if err != nil {
    83‚Üí		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
    84‚Üí			slog.Info("Project hash directory doesn't exist yet, will watch for it", "hashDir", w.hashDir)
    85‚Üí		} else {
    86‚Üí			slog.Warn("Cannot access project hash directory", "hashDir", w.hashDir, "error", err)
    87‚Üí		}
    88‚Üí		// Start watching for the directory to be created
    89‚Üí		w.wg.Add(1)
    90‚Üí		go w.watchForDirectory()
    91‚Üí		return nil
    92‚Üí	}
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí	// Log that we found the directory
    95‚Üí	slog.Info("Project hash directory exists", "hashDir", w.hashDir, "isDir", stat.IsDir())
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí	// Directory exists, start watching sessions
    98‚Üí	w.wg.Add(1)
    99‚Üí	go w.watchLoop()
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí	return nil
   102‚Üí}
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí// Stop gracefully stops the watcher
   105‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) Stop() {
   106‚Üí	slog.Info("Stopping Cursor watcher")
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí	// Perform one final check before stopping to catch any last-minute changes
   109‚Üí	slog.Info("Performing final check for changes before stopping")
   110‚Üí	w.checkForChanges()
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí	// Now stop the watcher goroutines
   113‚Üí	w.cancel()
   114‚Üí	w.wg.Wait()
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí	// Wait for any pending callback goroutines to complete
   117‚Üí	slog.Info("Waiting for pending callbacks to complete")
   118‚Üí	w.callbackWg.Wait()
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí	slog.Info("Cursor watcher stopped")
   121‚Üí}
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí// watchForDirectory waits for the project hash directory to be created
   124‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) watchForDirectory() {
   125‚Üí	defer w.wg.Done()
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí	ticker := time.NewTicker(w.pollInterval)
   128‚Üí	defer ticker.Stop()
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí	for {
   131‚Üí		select {
   132‚Üí		case <-w.ctx.Done():
   133‚Üí			return
   134‚Üí		case <-ticker.C:
   135‚Üí			// Check if directory now exists
   136‚Üí			if _, err := os.Stat(w.hashDir); err == nil {
   137‚Üí				slog.Info("Project hash directory detected, starting session watcher", "hashDir", w.hashDir)
   138‚Üí				// Directory exists now, start watching sessions
   139‚Üí				w.wg.Add(1)
   140‚Üí				go w.watchLoop()
   141‚Üí				return
   142‚Üí			}
   143‚Üí		}
   144‚Üí	}
   145‚Üí}
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí// watchLoop is the main monitoring loop
   148‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) watchLoop() {
   149‚Üí	defer w.wg.Done()
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí	ticker := time.NewTicker(w.pollInterval)
   152‚Üí	defer ticker.Stop()
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí	// Do an initial check immediately
   155‚Üí	w.checkForChanges()
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí	for {
   158‚Üí		select {
   159‚Üí		case <-w.ctx.Done():
   160‚Üí			return
   161‚Üí		case <-ticker.C:
   162‚Üí			// Uncomment this to see the ticker in action when debugging the watcher
   163‚Üí			//slog.Debug("Watcher tick - checking for changes")
   164‚Üí			w.checkForChanges()
   165‚Üí		}
   166‚Üí	}
   167‚Üí}
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí// checkForChanges scans all session directories for database changes
   170‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) checkForChanges() {
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí	// Get all session directories
   173‚Üí	sessionIDs, err := GetCursorSessionDirs(w.hashDir)
   174‚Üí	if err != nil {
   175‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to get session directories", "error", err)
   176‚Üí		return
   177‚Üí	}
   178‚Üí
   179‚Üí	for _, sessionID := range sessionIDs {
   180‚Üí		// Check if this is a NEW session or the resumed session
   181‚Üí		w.mu.RLock()
   182‚Üí		isKnown := w.knownSessions[sessionID]
   183‚Üí		isResumed := sessionID == w.resumedSession
   184‚Üí		w.mu.RUnlock()
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üí		// Skip known sessions unless it's the one being resumed
   187‚Üí		if isKnown && !isResumed {
   188‚Üí			continue
   189‚Üí		}
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí		// Check if this session has a store.db file
   192‚Üí		dbPath := filepath.Join(w.hashDir, sessionID, "store.db")
   193‚Üí
   194‚Üí		// Check if database exists
   195‚Üí		fileInfo, err := os.Stat(dbPath)
   196‚Üí		if err != nil {
   197‚Üí			continue // Skip sessions without store.db
   198‚Üí		}
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí		// For NEW sessions, process them once and then watch for changes
   201‚Üí		if !isKnown {
   202‚Üí			// Check if we've already seen this new session
   203‚Üí			if w.hasSessionChanged(sessionID, fileInfo, dbPath) {
   204‚Üí				slog.Info("Detected NEW Cursor session", "sessionId", sessionID)
   205‚Üí				w.processSessionChanges(sessionID, dbPath)
   206‚Üí			}
   207‚Üí		} else if isResumed {
   208‚Üí			// For resumed session, check for changes
   209‚Üí			slog.Debug("Polling resumed session for changes", "sessionId", sessionID)
   210‚Üí			if w.hasSessionChanged(sessionID, fileInfo, dbPath) {
   211‚Üí				slog.Info("Detected changes in resumed Cursor session", "sessionId", sessionID)
   212‚Üí				w.processSessionChanges(sessionID, dbPath)
   213‚Üí			} else {
   214‚Üí				slog.Debug("No changes detected in resumed session", "sessionId", sessionID)
   215‚Üí			}
   216‚Üí		}
   217‚Üí	}
   218‚Üí}
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí// hasSessionChanged checks if a session database has new records
   221‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) hasSessionChanged(sessionID string, fileInfo os.FileInfo, dbPath string) bool {
   222‚Üí	w.mu.Lock()
   223‚Üí	defer w.mu.Unlock()
   224‚Üí
   225‚Üí	// Always check record count - don't rely on file modification time
   226‚Üí	// SQLite with WAL mode may not update file mtime when records are added
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí	// Open database to count records (with WAL mode)
   229‚Üí	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath+"?mode=ro")
   230‚Üí	if err != nil {
   231‚Üí		slog.Error("Failed to open database", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   232‚Üí		return false
   233‚Üí	}
   234‚Üí	defer func() {
   235‚Üí		if err := db.Close(); err != nil {
   236‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to close database", "error", err)
   237‚Üí		}
   238‚Üí	}()
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí	// Enable WAL mode for non-blocking reads
   241‚Üí	if _, err := db.Exec("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL"); err != nil {
   242‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to enable WAL mode in watcher", "error", err)
   243‚Üí	}
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí	// Count total records in the blobs table
   246‚Üí	var count int
   247‚Üí	err = db.QueryRow("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM blobs").Scan(&count)
   248‚Üí	if err != nil {
   249‚Üí		slog.Error("Failed to count blobs", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   250‚Üí		return false
   251‚Üí	}
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üí	// Check if record count changed
   254‚Üí	lastCount, countExists := w.lastCounts[sessionID]
   255‚Üí	w.lastCounts[sessionID] = count
   256‚Üí
   257‚Üí	if !countExists {
   258‚Üí		// First time seeing this session - only process if it has content
   259‚Üí		if count > 0 {
   260‚Üí			slog.Debug("First check of session with content", "sessionId", sessionID, "recordCount", count)
   261‚Üí			return true
   262‚Üí		}
   263‚Üí		slog.Debug("First check of session but empty", "sessionId", sessionID)
   264‚Üí		return false
   265‚Üí	}
   266‚Üí
   267‚Üí	if count > lastCount {
   268‚Üí		// Records added to existing session
   269‚Üí		slog.Debug("Session has new records", "sessionId", sessionID, "oldCount", lastCount, "newCount", count)
   270‚Üí		return true
   271‚Üí	}
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí	// Uncomment this to see the ticker in action when debugging the watcher
   274‚Üí	//slog.Debug("No new records in session", "sessionId", sessionID, "count", count)
   275‚Üí	return false
   276‚Üí}
   277‚Üí
   278‚Üí// processSessionChanges handles changes detected in a session
   279‚Üífunc (w *CursorWatcher) processSessionChanges(sessionID string, dbPath string) {
   280‚Üí	slog.Info("Processing Cursor session changes", "sessionId", sessionID)
   281‚Üí
   282‚Üí	// Read the session data
   283‚Üí	sessionPath := filepath.Dir(dbPath) // Get the session directory from db path
   284‚Üí	createdAt, slug, blobRecords, _, err := ReadSessionData(sessionPath)
   285‚Üí	if err != nil {
   286‚Üí		slog.Error("Failed to read session data", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   287‚Üí		return
   288‚Üí	}
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí	if len(blobRecords) == 0 {
   291‚Üí		slog.Debug("Session has no message records", "sessionId", sessionID)
   292‚Üí		return
   293‚Üí	}
   294‚Üí
   295‚Üí	// Generate SessionData from blob records
   296‚Üí	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(blobRecords, w.projectPath, sessionID, createdAt, slug, w.tsCache)
   297‚Üí	if err != nil {
   298‚Üí		slog.Error("Failed to generate SessionData", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   299‚Üí		return
   300‚Üí	}
   301‚Üí
   302‚Üí	// Marshal blob records to JSON for raw data
   303‚Üí	rawDataJSON, err := json.Marshal(blobRecords)
   304‚Üí	if err != nil {
   305‚Üí		slog.Error("Failed to marshal blob records", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   306‚Üí		return
   307‚Üí	}
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí	// Write provider-specific debug output if requested
   310‚Üí	if w.debugRaw {
   311‚Üí		if err := writeDebugOutput(sessionID, string(rawDataJSON), nil); err != nil {
   312‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to write debug output", "sessionID", sessionID, "error", err)
   313‚Üí			// Don't fail the operation if debug output fails
   314‚Üí		}
   315‚Üí	}
   316‚Üí
   317‚Üí	// Create the AgentChatSession
   318‚Üí	agentSession := &spi.AgentChatSession{
   319‚Üí		SessionID:   sessionID,
   320‚Üí		CreatedAt:   createdAt,
   321‚Üí		Slug:        slug,
   322‚Üí		SessionData: sessionData,
   323‚Üí		RawData:     string(rawDataJSON),
   324‚Üí	}
   325‚Üí
   326‚Üí	// Call the callback asynchronously to avoid blocking the watcher
   327‚Üí	if w.sessionCallback != nil {
   328‚Üí		w.callbackWg.Add(1)
   329‚Üí		go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   330‚Üí			defer w.callbackWg.Done()
   331‚Üí			defer func() {
   332‚Üí				if r := recover(); r != nil {

## Complete Flow Analysis: SessionData ‚Üí Agent Event ‚Üí Provenance Push ‚Üí File Event Matching

I've thoroughly explored the provenance system in the codebase. Here's the complete flow and all relevant files:

---

### 1. **Agent Event Struct Definition and Validation**

**File: `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance/types.go`**

The `AgentEvent` struct (lines 76-88) represents a file operation reported by an AI agent:

```go
type AgentEvent struct {
	ID            string    // Deterministic ID for deduplication
	FilePath      string    // Path the agent touched (may be relative)
	ChangeType    string    // "create", "edit", "write", "delete"
	Timestamp     time.Time // When the operation was recorded
	SessionID     string    // Agent session ID
	ExchangeID    string    // Specific exchange within session
	MessageID     string    // Agent message ID
	AgentType     string    // "claude-code", "cursor", etc.
	AgentModel    string    // "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", etc.
	ActorHost     string    // Machine hostname
	ActorUsername string    // OS user
}
```

Validation (lines 90-117) requires:
- ID (non-empty)
- FilePath (non-empty, relative or absolute allowed)
- ChangeType (must be: "create", "edit", "write", or "delete")
- **Timestamp (required - checked with `Timestamp.IsZero()` on line 104)**
- SessionID (non-empty)
- ExchangeID (non-empty)
- AgentType (non-empty)

The error `ErrMissingTimestamp` is defined on line 17 and checked on line 104.

---

### 2. **How Agent Events are Extracted from SessionData and Pushed to Provenance**

**File: `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance/agent.go`**

**Extraction** (lines 44-89 - `ExtractAgentEvents` function):

The function walks through `SessionData.Exchanges` and `Exchange.Messages`:
1. **Filters for file-modifying tools only**: `schema.ToolTypeWrite`, `schema.ToolTypeShell`, `schema.ToolTypeGeneric` (lines 14-18)
2. **Skips read/search/task tools** (line 53)
3. **Requires completed tool output** (line 59): `msg.Tool.Output != nil` - only tool_result not tool_use
4. **Requires path hints** (line 62): Only messages with `PathHints` generate events
5. **Timestamp handling** (lines 66-70):
   - Uses message `Timestamp` if present
   - Falls back to `exchange.StartTime` if message timestamp is missing
6. **Creates one AgentEvent per PathHint** (lines 72-84):
   - Deterministic ID from SHA256 of sessionID, exchangeID, messageID, path
   - FilePath from PathHints
   - ChangeType always "write"
   - Maps `sessionData.Provider.ID` to AgentType
   - Maps `msg.Model` to AgentModel

**Pushing to Engine** (lines 94-112 - `PushAgentEvents` function):

```go
func PushAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, events []AgentEvent) []*ProvenanceRecord {
	var records []*ProvenanceRecord

	for _, event := range events {
		record, err := engine.PushAgentEvent(ctx, event)
		if err != nil {
			slog.Warn("Failed to push agent event", ...)
			continue
		}
		if record != nil {
			records = append(records, record)
		}
	}
	return records
}
```

**High-level orchestration** (lines 117-132 - `ProcessSessionEvents`):
- Extracts agent events from session data
- Pushes them to engine
- Returns any resulting provenance records

**Usage in main.go** (lines 100-105):
```go
func processProvenanceEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
	if engine == nil || session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
		return
	}
	provenance.ProcessSessionEvents(ctx, engine, session.SessionData)
}
```

---

### 3. **SessionData Structure**

**File: `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema/types.go`**

SessionData (lines 31-40) is the unified format from all agent providers:

```go
type SessionData struct {
	SchemaVersion string       `json:"schemaVersion"`
	Provider      ProviderInfo `json:"provider"`
	SessionID     string       `json:"sessionId"`
	CreatedAt     string       `json:"createdAt"`
	UpdatedAt     string       `json:"updatedAt,omitempty"`
	Slug          string       `json:"slug,omitempty"`
	WorkspaceRoot string       `json:"workspaceRoot"`
	Exchanges     []Exchange   `json:"exchanges"`
}
```

The nested structure contains:
- **ProviderInfo** (lines 43-47): ID, Name, Version of the agent
- **Exchange** (lines 50-56): ExchangeID, StartTime, EndTime, Messages
- **Message** (lines 59-68): ID, Timestamp, Role (user/agent), Model, Content, Tool, PathHints
- **ToolInfo** (lines 77-95): Name, Type (write/read/search/shell/task/generic/unknown), Input, Output

---

### 4. **The Provenance Engine and File Event Matching**

**File: `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance/engine.go`**

The Engine correlates file events to agent activity:

**Engine struct** (lines 42-46):
```go
type Engine struct {
	dbPath      string
	store       *Store
	matchWindow time.Duration
}
```

Default match window: 5 seconds (line 14)

**PushAgentEvent** (lines 134-183):
1. Validates agent event
2. Stores it in SQLite (via `store.PushAgentEvent`)
3. Normalizes the agent path (backslashes ‚Üí forward slashes, adds leading `/`)
4. Queries unmatched file events within ¬±matchWindow
5. Finds the **closest timestamp match** via `findBestMatch` (lines 187-207)
6. Uses **path suffix matching** via `pathSuffixMatch` (lines 212-222) to match paths at directory boundaries
7. Creates and marks both events as matched via `SetMatchedWith`
8. Returns a ProvenanceRecord

**Path Suffix Matching** (lines 212-222):
- Matches agent paths against FS paths as suffixes
- Requires directory boundary alignment (not mid-filename matches)
- Example: `/project/src/foo.go` matches `/src/foo.go` because `/` is the boundary

**ProvenanceRecord** (types.go lines 129-149):
```go
type ProvenanceRecord struct {
	FilePath      string    // Absolute path
	ChangeType    string    // from FileEvent
	Timestamp     time.Time // When file changed
	SessionID     string    // From AgentEvent
	ExchangeID    string    // From AgentEvent
	AgentType     string    // From AgentEvent
	AgentModel    string    // From AgentEvent
	MessageID     string    // For tracing
	ActorHost     string    // From AgentEvent
	ActorUsername string    // From AgentEvent
	MatchedAt     time.Time // When correlation occurred
}
```

---

### 5. **The SQLite Store**

**File: `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance/store.go`**

**Schema** (lines 69-82):
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	type TEXT NOT NULL,
	file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
	timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL,
	matched_with TEXT,
	payload TEXT NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_unmatched
	ON events(type, timestamp) WHERE matched_with IS NULL;
```

**Key operations**:
- `PushFileEvent(ctx, event)` - Stores file event with validation
- `PushAgentEvent(ctx, event)` - Stores agent event with path normalization
- `QueryUnmatchedFileEvents(ctx, since, until)` - Gets unmatched file events within time range
- `QueryUnmatchedAgentEvents(ctx, since, until)` - Gets unmatched agent events within time range
- `SetMatchedWith(ctx, eventID, matchedWithID)` - Marks both events as matched (bidirectional)

Uses WAL mode and `INSERT OR IGNORE` to handle duplicates safely.

---

### 6. **Filesystem Watcher**

**File: `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance/fswatcher.go`**

The FSWatcher monitors project directories and pushes FileEvents to the engine:

**FileEvent struct** (types.go lines 28-33):
```go
type FileEvent struct {
	ID         string    // Unique identifier
	Path       string    // Absolute path, forward slashes
	ChangeType string    // "create", "modify", "delete", "rename"
	Timestamp  time.Time // When the file changed (file ModTime)
}
```

**Key features**:
- **Exclusions** (lines 21-51): Hardcoded excluded dirs (.git, .specstory, node_modules, etc.)
- **Gitignore support** (lines 145-157, 314-327): Loads .gitignore from root and subdirectories
- **Intentignore support** (line 113): Project-specific Intent patterns
- **Debouncing** (lines 278-289): 100ms window to prevent duplicate rapid writes
- **File type filtering** (lines 53-72): Excludes binaries, .swp, .log, etc.
- **fsnotify integration** (lines 178-200): Converts fsnotify.Op to provenance change types:
  - Create ‚Üí "create"
  - Write ‚Üí "modify"
  - Remove ‚Üí "delete"
  - Rename ‚Üí "rename"

---

### 7. **Complete Integration Example**

From main.go (lines 57-105):

```go
// 1. Create provenance engine (if --provenance enabled)
engine, cleanup, err := startProvenanceEngine()

// 2. Start filesystem watcher
stopWatch, err := startProvenanceFSWatcher(ctx, engine, projectPath)

// 3. For each agent session, process provenance events
processProvenanceEvents(ctx, engine, session)
  // ‚Üí calls provenance.ProcessSessionEvents(ctx, engine, session.SessionData)
    // ‚Üí extracts agent events via ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)
    // ‚Üí pushes via PushAgentEvents(ctx, engine, events)
      // ‚Üí each event: engine.PushAgentEvent(ctx, event)
        // ‚Üí stores in SQLite
        // ‚Üí queries unmatched file events within ¬±5s
        // ‚Üí finds best match by timestamp + path suffix
        // ‚Üí creates ProvenanceRecord
        // ‚Üí marks both as matched

// 4. Cleanup
cleanup()
stopWatch()
```

   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí// NormalizePath normalizes an agent file path for storage and matching.
   120‚Üí// Backslashes are replaced with forward slashes, and a leading "/" is added if missing.
   121‚Üífunc NormalizePath(path string) string {
   122‚Üí	path = strings.ReplaceAll(path, "\\", "/")
   123‚Üí	if !strings.HasPrefix(path, "/") {
   124‚Üí		path = "/" + path
   125‚Üí	}
   126‚Üí	return path
   127‚Üí}
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí// ProvenanceRecord is the output of correlation ‚Äî a file change attributed to an agent exchange.
   130‚Üítype ProvenanceRecord struct {
   131‚Üí	// File data
   132‚Üí	FilePath   string    // Absolute path, forward slashes
   133‚Üí	ChangeType string    // "create", "modify", "delete", "rename"
   134‚Üí	Timestamp  time.Time // When file changed
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí	// Attribution
   137‚Üí	SessionID  string // Agent session that caused the change
   138‚Üí	ExchangeID string // Specific exchange that caused the change
   139‚Üí	AgentType  string // "claude-code", "cursor", etc.
   140‚Üí	AgentModel string // Model used
   141‚Üí	MessageID  string // For tracing
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí	// Actor info
   144‚Üí	ActorHost     string
   145‚Üí	ActorUsername string
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí	// Correlation metadata
   148‚Üí	MatchedAt time.Time // When correlation occurred
   149‚Üí}
   150‚Üí
