```text
     1‚Üípackage provenance
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"crypto/sha256"
     6‚Üí	"fmt"
     7‚Üí	"log/slog"
     8‚Üí	"time"
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
    11‚Üí)
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí// fileModifyingTools are tool types that can modify files and should generate agent events.
    14‚Üívar fileModifyingTools = map[string]bool{
    15‚Üí	schema.ToolTypeWrite:   true,
    16‚Üí	schema.ToolTypeShell:   true,
    17‚Üí	schema.ToolTypeGeneric: true,
    18‚Üí}
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí// deterministicID generates a stable ID from the given components so that
    21‚Üí// re-processing the same session produces the same events (deduped via INSERT OR IGNORE).
    22‚Üífunc deterministicID(parts ...string) string {
    23‚Üí	h := sha256.New()
    24‚Üí	for _, p := range parts {
    25‚Üí		h.Write([]byte(p))
    26‚Üí	}
    27‚Üí	return fmt.Sprintf("%x", h.Sum(nil))
    28‚Üí}
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí// parseTimestamp attempts to parse an RFC3339 timestamp string.
    31‚Üí// Returns the parsed time or the zero value if parsing fails.
    32‚Üífunc parseTimestamp(ts string) time.Time {
    33‚Üí	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ts)
    34‚Üí	if err != nil {
    35‚Üí		// Try RFC3339Nano as a fallback
    36‚Üí		t, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, ts)
    37‚Üí	}
    38‚Üí	return t
    39‚Üí}
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí// ExtractAgentEvents walks a SessionData's exchanges and messages, producing
    42‚Üí// one AgentEvent per PathHint on file-modifying tool uses (write, shell, generic).
    43‚Üí// Non-modifying tool types (read, search, task, unknown) are skipped.
    44‚Üífunc ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData *schema.SessionData) []AgentEvent {
    45‚Üí	if sessionData == nil {
    46‚Üí		return nil
    47‚Üí	}
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí	var events []AgentEvent
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí	for _, exchange := range sessionData.Exchanges {
    52‚Üí		for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
    53‚Üí			if msg.Tool == nil || !fileModifyingTools[msg.Tool.Type] {
    54‚Üí				continue
    55‚Üí			}
    56‚Üí			// Only emit events for completed tool results ‚Äî the tool_use timestamp
    57‚Üí			// is when the agent requested the write, which may be much earlier than
    58‚Üí			// the actual file change (e.g., waiting for user permission).
    59‚Üí			if msg.Tool.Output == nil {
    60‚Üí				continue
    61‚Üí			}
    62‚Üí			if len(msg.PathHints) == 0 {
    63‚Üí				continue
    64‚Üí			}
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí			ts := parseTimestamp(msg.Timestamp)
    67‚Üí			// Fall back to exchange start time if message has no timestamp
    68‚Üí			if ts.IsZero() {
    69‚Üí				ts = parseTimestamp(exchange.StartTime)
    70‚Üí			}
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí			for _, path := range msg.PathHints {
    73‚Üí				events = append(events, AgentEvent{
    74‚Üí					ID:         deterministicID(sessionData.SessionID, exchange.ExchangeID, msg.ID, path),
    75‚Üí					FilePath:   path,
    76‚Üí					ChangeType: "write",
    77‚Üí					Timestamp:  ts,
    78‚Üí					SessionID:  sessionData.SessionID,
    79‚Üí					ExchangeID: exchange.ExchangeID,
    80‚Üí					MessageID:  msg.ID,
    81‚Üí					AgentType:  sessionData.Provider.ID,
    82‚Üí					AgentModel: msg.Model,
    83‚Üí				})
    84‚Üí			}
    85‚Üí		}
    86‚Üí	}
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí	return events
    89‚Üí}
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí// PushAgentEvents pushes a slice of agent events to the engine and returns
    92‚Üí// any ProvenanceRecords produced by matches. Errors on individual events
    93‚Üí// are logged but do not stop processing.
    94‚Üífunc PushAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, events []AgentEvent) []*ProvenanceRecord {
    95‚Üí	var records []*ProvenanceRecord
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí	for _, event := range events {
    98‚Üí		record, err := engine.PushAgentEvent(ctx, event)
    99‚Üí		if err != nil {
   100‚Üí			slog.Warn("Failed to push agent event",
   101‚Üí				"eventID", event.ID,
   102‚Üí				"filePath", event.FilePath,
   103‚Üí				"error", err)
   104‚Üí			continue
   105‚Üí		}
   106‚Üí		if record != nil {
   107‚Üí			records = append(records, record)
   108‚Üí		}
   109‚Üí	}
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí	return records
   112‚Üí}
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí// ProcessSessionEvents extracts agent events from session data, pushes them to
   115‚Üí// the engine, and returns any resulting provenance records.
   116‚Üí// Safe to call with a nil engine ‚Äî returns nil immediately.
   117‚Üífunc ProcessSessionEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, sessionData *schema.SessionData) []*ProvenanceRecord {
   118‚Üí	if engine == nil || sessionData == nil {
   119‚Üí		return nil
   120‚Üí	}
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üí	events := ExtractAgentEvents(sessionData)
   123‚Üí	if len(events) == 0 {
   124‚Üí		return nil
   125‚Üí	}
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí	slog.Debug("Extracted agent events from session",
   128‚Üí		"sessionID", sessionData.SessionID,
   129‚Üí		"eventCount", len(events))
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí	return PushAgentEvents(ctx, engine, events)
   132‚Üí}
   133‚Üí
```

```text
     1‚Üípackage provenance

     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"encoding/json"

    10‚Üí	"strings"
    11‚Üí	"time"
    12‚Üí)
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üíconst defaultMatchWindow = 5 * time.Second
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí// defaultDBPath returns ~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db.
    17‚Üí// Returns an error if the home directory cannot be determined.
    18‚Üífunc defaultDBPath() (string, error) {
    19‚Üí	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
    20‚Üí	if err != nil {
    21‚Üí		return "", fmt.Errorf("determining home directory: %w", err)
    22‚Üí	}
    23‚Üí	return filepath.Join(home, ".specstory", "provenance", "provenance.db"), nil
    24‚Üí}
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí// Option configures the Engine.
    27‚Üítype Option func(*Engine)
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí// WithDBPath overrides the default database location (~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db).
    30‚Üífunc WithDBPath(path string) Option {
    31‚Üí	return func(e *Engine) { e.dbPath = path }
    32‚Üí}
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí// WithMatchWindow sets the time window for correlating file events to agent events.
    35‚Üí// A file event and agent event match only if their timestamps are within ¬±window.
    36‚Üí// Defaults to 5 seconds.
    37‚Üífunc WithMatchWindow(d time.Duration) Option {
    38‚Üí	return func(e *Engine) { e.matchWindow = d }
    39‚Üí}
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí// Engine correlates file events to agent activity and produces provenance records.
    42‚Üítype Engine struct {
    43‚Üí	dbPath      string
    44‚Üí	store       *Store
    45‚Üí	matchWindow time.Duration
    46‚Üí}
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí// NewEngine creates a correlation engine backed by a SQLite database.
    49‚Üí// By default the database is stored at ~/.specstory/provenance/provenance.db.
    50‚Üí// Use WithDBPath to override.
    51‚Üífunc NewEngine(opts ...Option) (*Engine, error) {
    52‚Üí	e := &Engine{
    53‚Üí		matchWindow: defaultMatchWindow,
    54‚Üí	}
    55‚Üí	for _, opt := range opts {
    56‚Üí		opt(e)
    57‚Üí	}
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí	// Resolve the default DB path only if no override was provided.
    60‚Üí	if e.dbPath == "" {
    61‚Üí		path, err := defaultDBPath()
    62‚Üí		if err != nil {
    63‚Üí			return nil, err
    64‚Üí		}
    65‚Üí		e.dbPath = path
    66‚Üí	}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí	store, err := OpenStore(e.dbPath)
    69‚Üí	if err != nil {
    70‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening store: %w", err)
    71‚Üí	}
    72‚Üí	e.store = store
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí	slog.Info("Provenance engine started",
    75‚Üí		"dbPath", e.dbPath,
    76‚Üí		"matchWindow", e.matchWindow)
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí	return e, nil
    79‚Üí}
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí// Close closes the underlying database connection.
    82‚Üífunc (e *Engine) Close() error {
    83‚Üí	return e.store.Close()
    84‚Üí}
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí// PushFileEvent stores a file event and attempts to correlate it with an existing
    87‚Üí// unmatched agent event. Returns a ProvenanceRecord if a match is found, nil otherwise.
    88‚Üífunc (e *Engine) PushFileEvent(ctx context.Context, event FileEvent) (*ProvenanceRecord, error) {
    89‚Üí	if err := e.store.PushFileEvent(ctx, event); err != nil {
    90‚Üí		return nil, err
    91‚Üí	}
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí	// Only fetch agent events within the match window ‚Äî events outside
    94‚Üí	// this range can never correlate, so there's no reason to load them.
    95‚Üí	since := event.Timestamp.Add(-e.matchWindow)
    96‚Üí	until := event.Timestamp.Add(e.matchWindow)
    97‚Üí	agentEvents, err := e.store.QueryUnmatchedAgentEvents(ctx, since, until)
    98‚Üí	if err != nil {
    99‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("querying unmatched agent events: %w", err)
   100‚Üí	}
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí	best := findBestMatch(event.Timestamp, agentEvents, e.matchWindow, func(agentPath string) bool {
   103‚Üí		return pathSuffixMatch(event.Path, agentPath)
   104‚Üí	})
   105‚Üí	if best == nil {
   106‚Üí		slog.Debug("No agent match for file event",
   107‚Üí			"fileEventID", event.ID,
   108‚Üí			"filePath", event.Path,
   109‚Üí			"candidates", len(agentEvents))
   110‚Üí		return nil, nil
   111‚Üí	}
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí	var agentEvt AgentEvent
   114‚Üí	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(best.Payload), &agentEvt); err != nil {
   115‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unmarshaling agent event payload: %w", err)
   116‚Üí	}
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí	delta := absDuration(event.Timestamp.Sub(best.Timestamp))
   119‚Üí	record := buildRecord(event, agentEvt)
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üí	if err := e.store.SetMatchedWith(ctx, event.ID, best.ID); err != nil {
   122‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting matched: %w", err)
   123‚Üí	}
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí	slog.Info("Matched file event to agent event",
   126‚Üí		"fileEventID", event.ID,
   127‚Üí		"agentEventID", best.ID,
   128‚Üí		"filePath", event.Path,
   129‚Üí		"delta", delta)
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí	return &record, nil
   132‚Üí}
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí// PushAgentEvent stores an agent event and attempts to correlate it with an existing
   135‚Üí// unmatched file event. Returns a ProvenanceRecord if a match is found, nil otherwise.
   136‚Üífunc (e *Engine) PushAgentEvent(ctx context.Context, event AgentEvent) (*ProvenanceRecord, error) {
   137‚Üí	if err := e.store.PushAgentEvent(ctx, event); err != nil {
   138‚Üí		return nil, err
   139‚Üí	}
   140‚Üí
   141‚Üí	normalizedPath := NormalizePath(event.FilePath)
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí	// Only fetch file events within the match window ‚Äî events outside
   144‚Üí	// this range can never correlate, so there's no reason to load them.
   145‚Üí	since := event.Timestamp.Add(-e.matchWindow)
   146‚Üí	until := event.Timestamp.Add(e.matchWindow)
   147‚Üí	fileEvents, err := e.store.QueryUnmatchedFileEvents(ctx, since, until)
   148‚Üí	if err != nil {
   149‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("querying unmatched file events: %w", err)
   150‚Üí	}
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí	bestFE := findBestMatch(event.Timestamp, fileEvents, e.matchWindow, func(fePath string) bool {
   153‚Üí		return pathSuffixMatch(fePath, normalizedPath)
   154‚Üí	})
   155‚Üí	if bestFE == nil {
   156‚Üí		slog.Debug("No file match for agent event",
   157‚Üí			"agentEventID", event.ID,
   158‚Üí			"agentPath", event.FilePath,
   159‚Üí			"candidates", len(fileEvents))
   160‚Üí		return nil, nil
   161‚Üí	}
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí	// Reconstruct the FileEvent from the stored payload
   164‚Üí	var fileEvt FileEvent
   165‚Üí	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(bestFE.Payload), &fileEvt); err != nil {
   166‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unmarshaling file event payload: %w", err)
   167‚Üí	}
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí	delta := absDuration(event.Timestamp.Sub(bestFE.Timestamp))
   170‚Üí	record := buildRecord(fileEvt, event)
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí	if err := e.store.SetMatchedWith(ctx, bestFE.ID, event.ID); err != nil {
   173‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting matched: %w", err)
   174‚Üí	}
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí	slog.Info("Matched agent event to file event",
   177‚Üí		"agentEventID", event.ID,
   178‚Üí		"fileEventID", bestFE.ID,
   179‚Üí		"filePath", bestFE.FilePath,
   180‚Üí		"delta", delta)
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí	return &record, nil
   183‚Üí}
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üí// findBestMatch finds the candidate closest in time whose path satisfies pathMatches.
   186‚Üí// Returns nil if no candidate matches within the window.
   187‚Üífunc findBestMatch(refTime time.Time, candidates []unmatchedEvent, window time.Duration, pathMatches func(candidatePath string) bool) *unmatchedEvent {
   188‚Üí	var best *unmatchedEvent
   189‚Üí	var bestDelta time.Duration
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí	for i := range candidates {
   192‚Üí		candidate := &candidates[i]
   193‚Üí		if !pathMatches(candidate.FilePath) {
   194‚Üí			continue
   195‚Üí		}
   196‚Üí		delta := absDuration(refTime.Sub(candidate.Timestamp))
   197‚Üí		if delta > window {
   198‚Üí			continue
   199‚Üí		}
   200‚Üí		if best == nil || delta < bestDelta {
   201‚Üí			best = candidate
   202‚Üí			bestDelta = delta
   203‚Üí		}
   204‚Üí	}
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí	return best
   207‚Üí}
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí// pathSuffixMatch returns true if the normalized agent path is a suffix of the FS path
   210‚Üí// aligned to a directory boundary. A raw suffix like "/foo.go" inside "/project/afoo.go"
   211‚Üí// is rejected because the match doesn't start at a "/" separator.
   212‚Üífunc pathSuffixMatch(fsPath, agentPath string) bool {
   213‚Üí	if !strings.HasSuffix(fsPath, agentPath) {
   214‚Üí		return false
   215‚Üí	}
   216‚Üí	if len(fsPath) == len(agentPath) {
   217‚Üí		return true
   218‚Üí	}
   219‚Üí	// The character just before the matched suffix must be '/' to ensure
   220‚Üí	// we're matching at a directory boundary, not mid-filename.
   221‚Üí	return fsPath[len(fsPath)-len(agentPath)] == '/'
   222‚Üí}
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üífunc absDuration(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
   225‚Üí	if d < 0 {
   226‚Üí		return -d
   227‚Üí	}
   228‚Üí	return d
   229‚Üí}
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí// buildRecord creates a ProvenanceRecord from a matched file event + agent event pair.
   232‚Üífunc buildRecord(fe FileEvent, ae AgentEvent) ProvenanceRecord {
   233‚Üí	return ProvenanceRecord{
   234‚Üí		FilePath:      fe.Path,
   235‚Üí		ChangeType:    fe.ChangeType,
   236‚Üí		Timestamp:     fe.Timestamp,
   237‚Üí		SessionID:     ae.SessionID,
   238‚Üí		ExchangeID:    ae.ExchangeID,
   239‚Üí		AgentType:     ae.AgentType,
   240‚Üí		AgentModel:    ae.AgentModel,
   241‚Üí		MessageID:     ae.MessageID,
   242‚Üí		ActorHost:     ae.ActorHost,
   243‚Üí		ActorUsername: ae.ActorUsername,
   244‚Üí		MatchedAt:     time.Now(),
   245‚Üí	}
   246‚Üí}
   247‚Üí
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pkg/provenance/store.go`

```text
     1‚Üípackage provenance
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"database/sql"
     6‚Üí	"encoding/json"
     7‚Üí	"fmt"
     8‚Üí	"os"
     9‚Üí	"path/filepath"
    10‚Üí	"time"
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí	_ "modernc.org/sqlite" // SQLite driver (pure Go, same as Intent)
    13‚Üí)
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí// Store handles SQLite persistence for file events and agent events.
    16‚Üí// It uses WAL mode for safe concurrent access from multiple processes.
    17‚Üítype Store struct {
    18‚Üí	db *sql.DB
    19‚Üí}
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí// OpenStore opens (or creates) a SQLite database at the given path.
    22‚Üí// Applies WAL mode and performance pragmas matching Intent's configuration.
    23‚Üífunc OpenStore(path string) (*Store, error) {
    24‚Üí	dir := filepath.Dir(path)
    25‚Üí	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
    26‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating database directory: %w", err)
    27‚Üí	}
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí	dsn := fmt.Sprintf("file:%s?_pragma=busy_timeout=15000&_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)", filepath.ToSlash(path))
    30‚Üí	db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
    31‚Üí	if err != nil {
    32‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening database: %w", err)
    33‚Üí	}
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí	// Serialize writes to avoid deadlocks (same pattern as Intent)
    36‚Üí	db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)
    37‚Üí	db.SetMaxIdleConns(1)
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí	// Performance pragmas matching Intent's sqlite.go
    40‚Üí	pragmas := []string{
    41‚Üí		"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL",
    42‚Üí		"PRAGMA cache_size = -64000",
    43‚Üí		"PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY",
    44‚Üí		"PRAGMA mmap_size = 268435456",
    45‚Üí		"PRAGMA page_size = 8192",
    46‚Üí	}
    47‚Üí	for _, p := range pragmas {
    48‚Üí		if _, err := db.Exec(p); err != nil {
    49‚Üí			_ = db.Close()
    50‚Üí			return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting pragma %q: %w", p, err)
    51‚Üí		}
    52‚Üí	}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí	s := &Store{db: db}
    55‚Üí	if err := s.ensureSchema(); err != nil {
    56‚Üí		_ = db.Close()
    57‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("ensuring schema: %w", err)
    58‚Üí	}
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí	return s, nil
    61‚Üí}
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí// Close closes the database connection.
    64‚Üífunc (s *Store) Close() error {
    65‚Üí	return s.db.Close()
    66‚Üí}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üífunc (s *Store) ensureSchema() error {
    69‚Üí	schema := `
    70‚Üí	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
    71‚Üí		id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    72‚Üí		type TEXT NOT NULL,
    73‚Üí		file_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    74‚Üí		timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL,
    75‚Üí		matched_with TEXT,
    76‚Üí		payload TEXT NOT NULL
    77‚Üí	);
    78‚Üí	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_unmatched
    79‚Üí		ON events(type, timestamp) WHERE matched_with IS NULL;
    80‚Üí	`
    81‚Üí	_, err := s.db.Exec(schema)
    82‚Üí	return err
    83‚Üí}
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí// PushFileEvent stores a file event for later correlation.
    86‚Üífunc (s *Store) PushFileEvent(ctx context.Context, event FileEvent) error {
    87‚Üí	if err := event.Validate(); err != nil {
    88‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("invalid file event: %w", err)
    89‚Üí	}
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí	payload, err := json.Marshal(event)
    92‚Üí	if err != nil {
    93‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("marshaling file event: %w", err)
    94‚Üí	}
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí	_, err = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
    97‚Üí		`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO events (id, type, file_path, timestamp, payload) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    98‚Üí		event.ID, "file_event", event.Path, event.Timestamp.UnixNano(), string(payload),
    99‚Üí	)
   100‚Üí	if err != nil {
   101‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("inserting file event: %w", err)
   102‚Üí	}
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí	return nil
   105‚Üí}
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí// PushAgentEvent stores an agent event for later correlation.
   108‚Üí// The file path is normalized before storage (backslashes ‚Üí forward slashes, leading / added).
   109‚Üífunc (s *Store) PushAgentEvent(ctx context.Context, event AgentEvent) error {
   110‚Üí	if err := event.Validate(); err != nil {
   111‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("invalid agent event: %w", err)
   112‚Üí	}
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí	normalizedPath := NormalizePath(event.FilePath)
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí	payload, err := json.Marshal(event)
   117‚Üí	if err != nil {
   118‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("marshaling agent event: %w", err)
   119‚Üí	}
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üí	_, err = s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
   122‚Üí		`INSERT OR IGNORE INTO events (id, type, file_path, timestamp, payload) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   123‚Üí		event.ID, "agent_event", normalizedPath, event.Timestamp.UnixNano(), string(payload),
   124‚Üí	)
   125‚Üí	if err != nil {
   126‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("inserting agent event: %w", err)
   127‚Üí	}
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí	return nil
   130‚Üí}
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí// unmatchedEvent is an internal row representation used during correlation.
   133‚Üítype unmatchedEvent struct {
   134‚Üí	ID        string
   135‚Üí	Type      string
   136‚Üí	FilePath  string
   137‚Üí	Timestamp time.Time
   138‚Üí	Payload   string
   139‚Üí}
   140‚Üí
   141‚Üí// QueryUnmatchedFileEvents returns file events that haven't been correlated yet
   142‚Üí// and fall within the given time range, ordered by timestamp ascending.
   143‚Üífunc (s *Store) QueryUnmatchedFileEvents(ctx context.Context, since, until time.Time) ([]unmatchedEvent, error) {
   144‚Üí	return s.queryUnmatched(ctx, "file_event", since, until)
   145‚Üí}
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí// QueryUnmatchedAgentEvents returns agent events that haven't been correlated yet
   148‚Üí// and fall within the given time range, ordered by timestamp ascending.
   149‚Üífunc (s *Store) QueryUnmatchedAgentEvents(ctx context.Context, since, until time.Time) ([]unmatchedEvent, error) {
   150‚Üí	return s.queryUnmatched(ctx, "agent_event", since, until)
   151‚Üí}
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üífunc (s *Store) queryUnmatched(ctx context.Context, eventType string, since, until time.Time) ([]unmatchedEvent, error) {
   154‚Üí	rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
   155‚Üí		`SELECT id, type, file_path, timestamp, payload
   156‚Üí		 FROM events
   157‚Üí		 WHERE type = ? AND matched_with IS NULL
   158‚Üí		   AND timestamp >= ? AND timestamp <= ?
   159‚Üí		 ORDER BY timestamp`,
   160‚Üí		eventType, since.UnixNano(), until.UnixNano(),
   161‚Üí	)
   162‚Üí	if err != nil {
   163‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("querying unmatched %s: %w", eventType, err)
   164‚Üí	}
   165‚Üí	defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí	var events []unmatchedEvent
   168‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   169‚Üí		var e unmatchedEvent
   170‚Üí		var nanos int64
   171‚Üí		if err := rows.Scan(&e.ID, &e.Type, &e.FilePath, &nanos, &e.Payload); err != nil {
   172‚Üí			return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanning row: %w", err)
   173‚Üí		}
   174‚Üí		e.Timestamp = time.Unix(0, nanos).UTC()
   175‚Üí		events = append(events, e)
   176‚Üí	}
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí	return events, rows.Err()
   179‚Üí}
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí// SetMatchedWith marks two events as correlated with each other in a single transaction.
   182‚Üífunc (s *Store) SetMatchedWith(ctx context.Context, eventID, matchedWithID string) error {
   183‚Üí	tx, err := s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
   184‚Üí	if err != nil {
   185‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("beginning transaction: %w", err)
   186‚Üí	}
   187‚Üí	defer func() { _ = tx.Rollback() }()
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí	_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
   190‚Üí		`UPDATE events SET matched_with = ? WHERE id = ?`,
   191‚Üí		matchedWithID, eventID,
   192‚Üí	)
   193‚Üí	if err != nil {
   194‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("updating event %s: %w", eventID, err)
   195‚Üí	}
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üí	_, err = tx.ExecContext(ctx,
   198‚Üí		`UPDATE events SET matched_with = ? WHERE id = ?`,
   199‚Üí		eventID, matchedWithID,
   200‚Üí	)
   201‚Üí	if err != nil {
   202‚Üí		return fmt.Errorf("updating event %s: %w", matchedWithID, err)
   203‚Üí	}
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí	return tx.Commit()
   206‚Üí}
   207‚Üí
```

```text
     1‚Üípackage provenance
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"log/slog"
     6‚Üí	"os"
     7‚Üí	"path/filepath"
     8‚Üí	"strings"
     9‚Üí	"sync"
    10‚Üí	"time"
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí	"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
    13‚Üí	"github.com/google/uuid"
    14‚Üí	ignore "github.com/sabhiram/go-gitignore"
    15‚Üí)
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíconst debounceWindow = 100 * time.Millisecond
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí// excludedDirs are directory basenames that should never be watched.
    20‚Üí// Checked via filepath.Base for O(1) lookup.
    21‚Üívar excludedDirs = map[string]bool{
    22‚Üí	".git":          true,
    23‚Üí	".specstory":    true,
    24‚Üí	"node_modules":  true,
    25‚Üí	".next":         true,
    26‚Üí	"__pycache__":   true,
    27‚Üí	".venv":         true,
    28‚Üí	"venv":          true,
    29‚Üí	"vendor":        true,
    30‚Üí	".idea":         true,
    31‚Üí	".vscode":       true,
    32‚Üí	"dist":          true,
    33‚Üí	".claude":       true,
    34‚Üí	".cursor":       true,
    35‚Üí	".codex":        true,
    36‚Üí	".aider":        true,
    37‚Üí	".copilot":      true,
    38‚Üí	".github":       true,
    39‚Üí	".gradle":       true,
    40‚Üí	".mvn":          true,
    41‚Üí	"build":         true,
    42‚Üí	"target":        true,
    43‚Üí	".terraform":    true,
    44‚Üí	".cache":        true,
    45‚Üí	".tox":          true,
    46‚Üí	".eggs":         true,
    47‚Üí	".mypy_cache":   true,
    48‚Üí	".pytest_cache": true,
    49‚Üí	".ruff_cache":   true,
    50‚Üí	"coverage":      true,
    51‚Üí}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí// excludedExtensions are file extensions that should never generate events.
    54‚Üívar excludedExtensions = map[string]bool{
    55‚Üí	".exe":   true,
    56‚Üí	".dll":   true,
    57‚Üí	".so":    true,
    58‚Üí	".dylib": true,
    59‚Üí	".o":     true,
    60‚Üí	".a":     true,
    61‚Üí	".out":   true,
    62‚Üí	".class": true,
    63‚Üí	".jar":   true,
    64‚Üí	".pyc":   true,
    65‚Üí	".pyo":   true,
    66‚Üí	".wasm":  true,
    67‚Üí	".swp":   true,
    68‚Üí	".swo":   true,
    69‚Üí	".tmp":   true,
    70‚Üí	".bak":   true,
    71‚Üí	".log":   true,
    72‚Üí}
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí// scopedIgnore pairs a directory with its compiled ignore patterns.
    75‚Üí// Patterns only apply to paths under the scoped directory.
    76‚Üítype scopedIgnore struct {
    77‚Üí	dir     string
    78‚Üí	matcher *ignore.GitIgnore
    79‚Üí}
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí// FSWatcher watches the user's project directory for filesystem changes and
    82‚Üí// pushes FileEvents to the provenance Engine for correlation with agent activity.
    83‚Üítype FSWatcher struct {
    84‚Üí	engine      *Engine
    85‚Üí	rootDir     string
    86‚Üí	watcher     *fsnotify.Watcher
    87‚Üí	ignoreRules []scopedIgnore // scoped .gitignore/.intentignore matchers
    88‚Üí	cancel      context.CancelFunc
    89‚Üí	wg          sync.WaitGroup
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí	// Debounce: track last event time per path
    92‚Üí	mu       sync.Mutex
    93‚Üí	lastSeen map[string]time.Time
    94‚Üí}
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí// NewFSWatcher creates a filesystem watcher rooted at rootDir that will push
    97‚Üí// FileEvents to the given engine. It loads .gitignore files from the root and
    98‚Üí// all subdirectories (plus .intentignore at root), then recursively adds all
    99‚Üí// non-excluded directories. The watcher is not running yet ‚Äî call Start to begin.
   100‚Üífunc NewFSWatcher(engine *Engine, rootDir string) (*FSWatcher, error) {
   101‚Üí	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   102‚Üí	if err != nil {
   103‚Üí		return nil, err
   104‚Üí	}
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí	w := &FSWatcher{
   107‚Üí		engine:   engine,
   108‚Üí		rootDir:  rootDir,
   109‚Üí		watcher:  watcher,
   110‚Üí		lastSeen: make(map[string]time.Time),
   111‚Üí	}
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí	// .intentignore is root-only (Intent-specific patterns)
   114‚Üí	w.loadIgnoreFile(rootDir, ".intentignore")
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí	// Recursively add all non-excluded directories, loading .gitignore
   117‚Üí	// files from each directory as we descend. WalkDir visits top-down,
   118‚Üí	// so parent patterns are available before child directories are filtered.
   119‚Üí	err = filepath.WalkDir(rootDir, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
   120‚Üí		if err != nil {
   121‚Üí			return nil // skip directories we can't read
   122‚Üí		}
   123‚Üí		if !d.IsDir() {
   124‚Üí			return nil
   125‚Üí		}
   126‚Üí		if !w.shouldWatch(path, true) {
   127‚Üí			return filepath.SkipDir
   128‚Üí		}
   129‚Üí		w.loadIgnoreFile(path, ".gitignore")
   130‚Üí		if addErr := watcher.Add(path); addErr != nil {
   131‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to watch directory", "path", path, "error", addErr)
   132‚Üí		}
   133‚Üí		return nil
   134‚Üí	})
   135‚Üí	if err != nil {
   136‚Üí		_ = watcher.Close()
   137‚Üí		return nil, err
   138‚Üí	}
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üí	slog.Info("FSWatcher initialized", "rootDir", rootDir)
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí	return w, nil
   143‚Üí}
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí// loadIgnoreFile reads an ignore file (e.g. ".gitignore", ".intentignore") from
   146‚Üí// dir and appends its patterns as a scoped rule. No-op if the file doesn't exist.
   147‚Üífunc (w *FSWatcher) loadIgnoreFile(dir, filename string) {
   148‚Üí	filePath := filepath.Join(dir, filename)
   149‚Üí	content, err := os.ReadFile(filePath)
   150‚Üí	if err != nil {
   151‚Üí		return
   152‚Üí	}
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí	lines := strings.Split(string(content), "\n")
   155‚Üí	matcher := ignore.CompileIgnoreLines(lines...)
   156‚Üí	w.ignoreRules = append(w.ignoreRules, scopedIgnore{dir: dir, matcher: matcher})
   157‚Üí}
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí// Start begins the event loop in a background goroutine.
   160‚Üí// The watcher runs until Stop is called or the context is cancelled.
   161‚Üífunc (w *FSWatcher) Start(ctx context.Context) {
   162‚Üí	ctx, w.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
   163‚Üí	w.wg.Add(1)
   164‚Üí	go w.eventLoop(ctx)
   165‚Üí	slog.Info("FSWatcher started", "rootDir", w.rootDir)
   166‚Üí}
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí// Stop cancels the event loop, waits for it to finish, and closes the watcher.
   169‚Üífunc (w *FSWatcher) Stop() {
   170‚Üí	if w.cancel != nil {
   171‚Üí		w.cancel()
   172‚Üí	}
   173‚Üí	w.wg.Wait()
   174‚Üí	_ = w.watcher.Close()
   175‚Üí	slog.Info("FSWatcher stopped", "rootDir", w.rootDir)
   176‚Üí}
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí// eventLoop processes fsnotify events until the context is cancelled.
   179‚Üífunc (w *FSWatcher) eventLoop(ctx context.Context) {
   180‚Üí	defer w.wg.Done()
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí	for {
   183‚Üí		select {
   184‚Üí		case <-ctx.Done():
   185‚Üí			return
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí		case event, ok := <-w.watcher.Events:
   188‚Üí			if !ok {
   189‚Üí				return
   190‚Üí			}
   191‚Üí			w.handleEvent(ctx, event)
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí		case err, ok := <-w.watcher.Errors:
   194‚Üí			if !ok {
   195‚Üí				return
   196‚Üí			}
   197‚Üí			slog.Debug("FSWatcher error", "error", err)
   198‚Üí		}
   199‚Üí	}
   200‚Üí}
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí// handleEvent processes a single fsnotify event, filtering and debouncing
   203‚Üí// before pushing a FileEvent to the engine.
   204‚Üífunc (w *FSWatcher) handleEvent(ctx context.Context, event fsnotify.Event) {
   205‚Üí	path := event.Name
   206‚Üí
   207‚Üí	// Determine if this is a directory (for create events, new dirs need watching)
   208‚Üí	info, statErr := os.Stat(path)
   209‚Üí	isDir := statErr == nil && info.IsDir()
   210‚Üí
   211‚Üí	if !w.shouldWatch(path, isDir) {
   212‚Üí		return
   213‚Üí	}
   214‚Üí
   215‚Üí	// For new directories, load any .gitignore and add to the watcher
   216‚Üí	if isDir && event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   217‚Üí		w.loadIgnoreFile(path, ".gitignore")
   218‚Üí		if addErr := w.watcher.Add(path); addErr != nil {
   219‚Üí			slog.Debug("Failed to watch new directory", "path", path, "error", addErr)
   220‚Üí		}
   221‚Üí		return // directories themselves don't produce FileEvents
   222‚Üí	}
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí	// Only file events from here on
   225‚Üí	if isDir {
   226‚Üí		return
   227‚Üí	}
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí	changeType := mapChangeType(event.Op)
   230‚Üí	if changeType == "" {
   231‚Üí		return
   232‚Üí	}
   233‚Üí
   234‚Üí	if w.shouldDebounce(path) {
   235‚Üí		return
   236‚Üí	}
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üí	// Determine timestamp: use file ModTime when possible, fall back to now
   239‚Üí	var timestamp time.Time
   240‚Üí	if statErr == nil {
   241‚Üí		timestamp = info.ModTime()
   242‚Üí	} else {
   243‚Üí		// File was deleted/renamed ‚Äî can't stat it
   244‚Üí		timestamp = time.Now()
   245‚Üí	}
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí	fileEvent := FileEvent{
   248‚Üí		ID:         uuid.New().String(),
   249‚Üí		Path:       path,
   250‚Üí		ChangeType: changeType,
   251‚Üí		Timestamp:  timestamp,
   252‚Üí	}
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí	if _, err := w.engine.PushFileEvent(ctx, fileEvent); err != nil {
   255‚Üí		slog.Debug("Failed to push file event",
   256‚Üí			"path", path,
   257‚Üí			"changeType", changeType,
   258‚Üí			"error", err)
   259‚Üí	}
   260‚Üí}
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí// mapChangeType converts fsnotify operations to provenance change types.
   263‚Üífunc mapChangeType(op fsnotify.Op) string {
   264‚Üí	switch {
   265‚Üí	case op.Has(fsnotify.Create):
   266‚Üí		return "create"
   267‚Üí	case op.Has(fsnotify.Write):
   268‚Üí		return "modify"
   269‚Üí	case op.Has(fsnotify.Remove):
   270‚Üí		return "delete"
   271‚Üí	case op.Has(fsnotify.Rename):
   272‚Üí		return "rename"
   273‚Üí	default:
   274‚Üí		return ""
   275‚Üí	}
   276‚Üí}
   277‚Üí
   278‚Üí// shouldDebounce returns true if the same path had an event less than
   279‚Üí// debounceWindow ago, preventing duplicate events from rapid writes.
   280‚Üífunc (w *FSWatcher) shouldDebounce(path string) bool {
   281‚Üí	w.mu.Lock()
   282‚Üí	defer w.mu.Unlock()
   283‚Üí	now := time.Now()
   284‚Üí	if last, ok := w.lastSeen[path]; ok && now.Sub(last) < debounceWindow {
   285‚Üí		return true
   286‚Üí	}
   287‚Üí	w.lastSeen[path] = now
   288‚Üí	return false
   289‚Üí}
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí// shouldWatch returns true if the given path should be watched/produce events.
   292‚Üí// Checks are ordered from cheapest to most expensive.
   293‚Üífunc (w *FSWatcher) shouldWatch(path string, isDir bool) bool {
   294‚Üí	base := filepath.Base(path)
   295‚Üí
   296‚Üí	// Hardcoded directory exclusions
   297‚Üí	if isDir && excludedDirs[base] {
   298‚Üí		return false
   299‚Üí	}
   300‚Üí
   301‚Üí	// Hidden files/directories (starts with .)
   302‚Üí	if strings.HasPrefix(base, ".") {
   303‚Üí		return false
   304‚Üí	}
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí	// Hardcoded file extension exclusions
   307‚Üí	if !isDir {
   308‚Üí		ext := strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(base))
   309‚Üí		if excludedExtensions[ext] {
   310‚Üí			return false
   311‚Üí		}
   312‚Üí	}
   313‚Üí
   314‚Üí	// Scoped ignore rules (.gitignore from each directory, .intentignore from root).
   315‚Üí	// Each rule only applies to paths under its directory.
   316‚Üí	for _, rule := range w.ignoreRules {
   317‚Üí		relPath, err := filepath.Rel(rule.dir, path)
   318‚Üí		if err != nil || strings.HasPrefix(relPath, "..") {
   319‚Üí			continue // path is not under this rule's directory
   320‚Üí		}
   321‚Üí		if isDir {
   322‚Üí			relPath = relPath + "/"
   323‚Üí		}
   324‚Üí		if rule.matcher.MatchesPath(relPath) {
   325‚Üí			return false
   326‚Üí		}
   327‚Üí	}
   328‚Üí
   329‚Üí	return true
   330‚Üí}
   331‚Üí
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1‚Üípackage provenance
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
```

```text
    10‚Üí// testEngine creates a temporary Engine for testing.
    11‚Üífunc testEngine(t *testing.T, opts ...Option) *Engine {
    12‚Üí	t.Helper()
    13‚Üí	path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "test.db")
    14‚Üí	opts = append([]Option{WithDBPath(path)}, opts...)
    15‚Üí	e, err := NewEngine(opts...)
    16‚Üí	if err != nil {
    17‚Üí		t.Fatalf("NewEngine: %v", err)
    18‚Üí	}
    19‚Üí	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = e.Close() })
    20‚Üí	return e
    21‚Üí}
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üífunc TestPathSuffixMatch(t *testing.T) {
    24‚Üí	// Cases from the plan's matching algorithm table
    25‚Üí	tests := []struct {
    26‚Üí		name      string
    27‚Üí		fsPath    string
    28‚Üí		agentPath string
    29‚Üí		want      bool
    30‚Üí	}{
    31‚Üí		{
    32‚Üí			name:      "relative file matches as suffix",
    33‚Üí			fsPath:    "/project/src/foo.go",
    34‚Üí			agentPath: "/foo.go",
    35‚Üí			want:      true,
    36‚Üí		},
    37‚Üí		{
    38‚Üí			name:      "relative with directory matches",
    39‚Üí			fsPath:    "/project/src/foo.go",
    40‚Üí			agentPath: "/src/foo.go",
    41‚Üí			want:      true,
    42‚Üí		},
    43‚Üí		{
    44‚Üí			name:      "exact absolute match",
    45‚Üí			fsPath:    "/project/src/foo.go",
    46‚Üí			agentPath: "/project/src/foo.go",
    47‚Üí			want:      true,
    48‚Üí		},
    49‚Üí		{
    50‚Üí			name:      "different filename no match",
    51‚Üí			fsPath:    "/project/src/foo.go",
    52‚Üí			agentPath: "/bar.go",
    53‚Üí			want:      false,
    54‚Üí		},
    55‚Üí		{
    56‚Üí			name:      "partial filename no match",
    57‚Üí			fsPath:    "/project/src/foobar.go",
    58‚Üí			agentPath: "/bar.go",
    59‚Üí			want:      false,
    60‚Üí		},
    61‚Üí		{
    62‚Üí			name:      "agent path suffix of longer filename rejected",
    63‚Üí			fsPath:    "/project/src/afoo.go",
    64‚Üí			agentPath: "/foo.go",
    65‚Üí			want:      false,
    66‚Üí		},
    67‚Üí		{
    68‚Üí			name:      "agent directory suffix of longer dirname rejected",
    69‚Üí			fsPath:    "/project/xsrc/foo.go",
    70‚Üí			agentPath: "/src/foo.go",
    71‚Üí			want:      false,
    72‚Üí		},
    73‚Üí	}
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí	for _, tt := range tests {
    76‚Üí		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
    77‚Üí			got := pathSuffixMatch(tt.fsPath, tt.agentPath)
    78‚Üí			if got != tt.want {
    79‚Üí				t.Errorf("pathSuffixMatch(%q, %q) = %v, want %v",
    80‚Üí					tt.fsPath, tt.agentPath, got, tt.want)
    81‚Üí			}
    82‚Üí		})
    83‚Üí	}
    84‚Üí}

    86‚Üífunc TestPushFileEvent_MatchesExistingAgent(t *testing.T) {
    87‚Üí	ctx := context.Background()
    88‚Üí	now := time.Date(2025, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
    89‚Üí	e := testEngine(t)
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí	// Push agent event first
    92‚Üí	_, err := e.PushAgentEvent(ctx, AgentEvent{
    93‚Üí		ID:         "ae-1",
    94‚Üí		FilePath:   "src/main.go",
    95‚Üí		ChangeType: "edit",
    96‚Üí		Timestamp:  now,
    97‚Üí		SessionID:  "sess-1",
    98‚Üí		ExchangeID: "exch-1",
    99‚Üí		AgentType:  "claude-code",
   100‚Üí		AgentModel: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
   101‚Üí	})
   102‚Üí	if err != nil {
   103‚Üí		t.Fatalf("PushAgentEvent: %v", err)
   104‚Üí	}
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí	// Push file event ‚Äî should match the agent event
   107‚Üí	record, err := e.PushFileEvent(ctx, FileEvent{
   108‚Üí		ID:         "fe-1",
   109‚Üí		Path:       "/project/src/main.go",
   110‚Üí		ChangeType: "modify",
   111‚Üí		Timestamp:  now.Add(1 * time.Second),
   112‚Üí	})
   113‚Üí	if err != nil {
   114‚Üí		t.Fatalf("PushFileEvent: %v", err)
   115‚Üí	}
   116‚Üí	if record == nil {
   117‚Üí		t.Fatal("expected a ProvenanceRecord, got nil")
   118‚Üí	}
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí	if record.FilePath != "/project/src/main.go" {
   121‚Üí		t.Errorf("FilePath = %q, want %q", record.FilePath, "/project/src/main.go")
   122‚Üí	}
   123‚Üí	if record.SessionID != "sess-1" {
   124‚Üí		t.Errorf("SessionID = %q, want %q", record.SessionID, "sess-1")
   125‚Üí	}
   126‚Üí	if record.ExchangeID != "exch-1" {
   127‚Üí		t.Errorf("ExchangeID = %q, want %q", record.ExchangeID, "exch-1")
   128‚Üí	}
   129‚Üí	if record.AgentType != "claude-code" {
   130‚Üí		t.Errorf("AgentType = %q, want %q", record.AgentType, "claude-code")
   131‚Üí	}
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí}

   217‚Üífunc TestPushFileEvent_TimeWindowBoundary(t *testing.T) {
   218‚Üí	// The match window boundary is exclusive: delta > window means no match,
   219‚Üí	// so exactly 5s matches but 5s+1ns does not.
   220‚Üí	tests := []struct {
   221‚Üí		name      string
   222‚Üí		delta     time.Duration
   223‚Üí		wantMatch bool
   224‚Üí	}{
   225‚Üí		{name: "exactly at window boundary matches", delta: 5 * time.Second, wantMatch: true},
   226‚Üí		{name: "1ns beyond window does not match", delta: 5*time.Second + 1*time.Nanosecond, wantMatch: false},
   227‚Üí		{name: "well outside window does not match", delta: 10 * time.Second, wantMatch: false},
   228‚Üí	}
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üí	for _, tt := range tests {
   231‚Üí		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   232‚Üí			ctx := context.Background()
   233‚Üí			now := time.Date(2025, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
   234‚Üí			e := testEngine(t)
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí			_, err := e.PushAgentEvent(ctx, AgentEvent{
   237‚Üí				ID:         "ae-1",
   238‚Üí				FilePath:   "foo.go",
   239‚Üí				ChangeType: "edit",
   240‚Üí				Timestamp:  now,
   241‚Üí				SessionID:  "sess-1",
   242‚Üí				ExchangeID: "exch-1",
   243‚Üí				AgentType:  "claude-code",
   244‚Üí			})
   245‚Üí			if err != nil {
   246‚Üí				t.Fatalf("PushAgentEvent: %v", err)
   247‚Üí			}
   248‚Üí
   249‚Üí			record, err := e.PushFileEvent(ctx, FileEvent{
   250‚Üí				ID:         "fe-1",
   251‚Üí				Path:       "/project/foo.go",
   252‚Üí				ChangeType: "modify",
   253‚Üí				Timestamp:  now.Add(tt.delta),
   254‚Üí			})
   255‚Üí			if err != nil {
   256‚Üí				t.Fatalf("PushFileEvent: %v", err)
   257‚Üí			}
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí			gotMatch := record != nil
   260‚Üí			if gotMatch != tt.wantMatch {
   261‚Üí				t.Errorf("delta=%v: got match=%v, want match=%v", tt.delta, gotMatch, tt.wantMatch)
   262‚Üí			}
   263‚Üí		})
   264‚Üí	}
   265‚Üí}

   267‚Üífunc TestPushFileEvent_ClosestTimestampWins(t *testing.T) {
   268‚Üí	ctx := context.Background()
   269‚Üí	now := time.Date(2025, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
   270‚Üí	e := testEngine(t)
   271‚Üí
   272‚Üí	// Two agent events for the same path at different times
   273‚Üí	_, err := e.PushAgentEvent(ctx, AgentEvent{
   274‚Üí		ID:         "ae-far",
   275‚Üí		FilePath:   "src/foo.go",
   276‚Üí		ChangeType: "edit",
   277‚Üí		Timestamp:  now.Add(-4 * time.Second),
   278‚Üí		SessionID:  "sess-far",
   279‚Üí		ExchangeID: "exch-far",
   280‚Üí		AgentType:  "claude-code",
   281‚Üí	})
   282‚Üí	if err != nil {
   283‚Üí		t.Fatalf("PushAgentEvent (far): %v", err)
   284‚Üí	}
   285‚Üí
   286‚Üí	_, err = e.PushAgentEvent(ctx, AgentEvent{
   287‚Üí		ID:         "ae-close",
   288‚Üí		FilePath:   "src/foo.go",
   289‚Üí		ChangeType: "edit",
   290‚Üí		Timestamp:  now.Add(1 * time.Second),
   291‚Üí		SessionID:  "sess-close",
   292‚Üí		ExchangeID: "exch-close",
   293‚Üí		AgentType:  "claude-code",
   294‚Üí	})
   295‚Üí	if err != nil {
   296‚Üí		t.Fatalf("PushAgentEvent (close): %v", err)
   297‚Üí	}
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí	// File event at T=0 ‚Äî closer to ae-close (1s) than ae-far (4s)
   300‚Üí	record, err := e.PushFileEvent(ctx, FileEvent{
   301‚Üí		ID:         "fe-1",
   302‚Üí		Path:       "/project/src/foo.go",
   303‚Üí		ChangeType: "modify",
   304‚Üí		Timestamp:  now,
   305‚Üí	})
   306‚Üí	if err != nil {
   307‚Üí		t.Fatalf("PushFileEvent: %v", err)
   308‚Üí	}
   309‚Üí	if record == nil {
   310‚Üí		t.Fatal("expected a record, got nil")
   311‚Üí	}
   312‚Üí	if record.SessionID != "sess-close" {
   313‚Üí		t.Errorf("SessionID = %q, want %q (closest match)", record.SessionID, "sess-close")
   314‚Üí	}
   315‚Üí}

   365‚Üífunc TestCustomMatchWindow(t *testing.T) {
   366‚Üí	ctx := context.Background()
   367‚Üí	now := time.Date(2025, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
   368‚Üí
   369‚Üí	// 2s window instead of default 5s
   370‚Üí	e := testEngine(t, WithMatchWindow(2*time.Second))
   371‚Üí
   372‚Üí	_, err := e.PushAgentEvent(ctx, AgentEvent{
   373‚Üí		ID:         "ae-1",
   374‚Üí		FilePath:   "foo.go",
   375‚Üí		ChangeType: "edit",
   376‚Üí		Timestamp:  now,
   377‚Üí		SessionID:  "sess-1",
   378‚Üí		ExchangeID: "exch-1",
   379‚Üí		AgentType:  "claude-code",
   380‚Üí	})
   381‚Üí	if err != nil {
   382‚Üí		t.Fatalf("PushAgentEvent: %v", err)
   383‚Üí	}
   384‚Üí
   385‚Üí	// 3s after agent event ‚Äî inside default 5s but outside custom 2s
   386‚Üí	record, err := e.PushFileEvent(ctx, FileEvent{
   387‚Üí		ID:         "fe-1",
   388‚Üí		Path:       "/project/foo.go",
   389‚Üí		ChangeType: "modify",
   390‚Üí		Timestamp:  now.Add(3 * time.Second),
   391‚Üí	})
   392‚Üí	if err != nil {
   393‚Üí		t.Fatalf("PushFileEvent: %v", err)
   394‚Üí	}
   395‚Üí	if record != nil {
   396‚Üí		t.Errorf("expected nil (outside 2s window), got %+v", record)
   397‚Üí	}
   398‚Üí}
```

```text
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üífunc TestAgentEvent_Validate(t *testing.T) {
   105‚Üí	now := time.Now()
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí	// validEvent returns a fully populated AgentEvent for tests to selectively zero out.
   108‚Üí	validEvent := func() AgentEvent {
   109‚Üí		return AgentEvent{
   110‚Üí			ID:         "agent-1",
   111‚Üí			FilePath:   "src/foo.go",
   112‚Üí			ChangeType: "edit",
   113‚Üí			Timestamp:  now,
   114‚Üí			SessionID:  "session-1",
   115‚Üí			ExchangeID: "exchange-1",
   116‚Üí			AgentType:  "claude-code",
   117‚Üí		}
   118‚Üí	}
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí	tests := []struct {
   121‚Üí		name    string
   122‚Üí		modify  func(*AgentEvent)
   123‚Üí		wantErr error
   124‚Üí	}{
   125‚Üí		{
   126‚Üí			name:    "valid event",
   127‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) {},
   128‚Üí			wantErr: nil,
   129‚Üí		},
   130‚Üí		{
   131‚Üí			name:    "missing ID",
   132‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.ID = "" },
   133‚Üí			wantErr: ErrMissingID,
   134‚Üí		},
   135‚Üí		{
   136‚Üí			name:    "missing file path",
   137‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.FilePath = "" },
   138‚Üí			wantErr: ErrMissingFilePath,
   139‚Üí		},
   140‚Üí		{
   141‚Üí			name:    "missing change type",
   142‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.ChangeType = "" },
   143‚Üí			wantErr: ErrMissingChangeType,
   144‚Üí		},
   145‚Üí		{
   146‚Üí			name:    "invalid change type rejected",
   147‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.ChangeType = "modify" },
   148‚Üí			wantErr: ErrInvalidAgentChangeType,
   149‚Üí		},
   150‚Üí		{
   151‚Üí			name:    "missing timestamp",
   152‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.Timestamp = time.Time{} },
   153‚Üí			wantErr: ErrMissingTimestamp,
   154‚Üí		},
   155‚Üí		{
   156‚Üí			name:    "missing session ID",
   157‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.SessionID = "" },
   158‚Üí			wantErr: ErrMissingSessionID,
   159‚Üí		},
   160‚Üí		{
   161‚Üí			name:    "missing exchange ID",
   162‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.ExchangeID = "" },
   163‚Üí			wantErr: ErrMissingExchangeID,
   164‚Üí		},
   165‚Üí		{
   166‚Üí			name:    "missing agent type",
   167‚Üí			modify:  func(e *AgentEvent) { e.AgentType = "" },
   168‚Üí			wantErr: ErrMissingAgentType,
   169‚Üí		},
   170‚Üí		{
   171‚Üí			name: "relative path is allowed",
   172‚Üí			modify: func(e *AgentEvent) {
   173‚Üí				e.FilePath = "relative/path.go"
   174‚Üí			},
   175‚Üí			wantErr: nil,
   176‚Üí		},
   177‚Üí	}
   178‚Üí
   179‚Üí	for _, tt := range tests {
   180‚Üí		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   181‚Üí			event := validEvent()
   182‚Üí			tt.modify(&event)
   183‚Üí			err := event.Validate()
   184‚Üí			if err != tt.wantErr {
   185‚Üí				t.Errorf("Validate() = %v, want %v", err, tt.wantErr)
   186‚Üí			}
   187‚Üí		})
   188‚Üí	}
   189‚Üí}
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üífunc TestNormalizePath(t *testing.T) {
   192‚Üí	tests := []struct {
   193‚Üí		name string
   194‚Üí		path string
   195‚Üí		want string
   196‚Üí	}{
   197‚Üí		{
   198‚Üí			name: "relative path gets leading slash",
   199‚Üí			path: "foo.go",
   200‚Üí			want: "/foo.go",
   201‚Üí		},
   202‚Üí		{
   203‚Üí			name: "relative path with directory",
   204‚Üí			path: "src/foo.go",
   205‚Üí			want: "/src/foo.go",
   206‚Üí		},
   207‚Üí		{
   208‚Üí			name: "absolute path unchanged",
   209‚Üí			path: "/project/src/foo.go",
   210‚Üí			want: "/project/src/foo.go",
   211‚Üí		},
   212‚Üí		{
   213‚Üí			name: "backslashes converted to forward slashes",
   214‚Üí			path: "src\\pkg\\foo.go",
   215‚Üí			want: "/src/pkg/foo.go",
   216‚Üí		},
   217‚Üí		{
   218‚Üí			name: "mixed separators normalized",
   219‚Üí			path: "src/pkg\\foo.go",
   220‚Üí			want: "/src/pkg/foo.go",
   221‚Üí		},
   222‚Üí		{
   223‚Üí			name: "absolute path with backslashes",
   224‚Üí			path: "\\project\\src\\foo.go",
   225‚Üí			want: "/project/src/foo.go",
   226‚Üí		},
   227‚Üí	}
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí	for _, tt := range tests {
   230‚Üí		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   231‚Üí			got := NormalizePath(tt.path)
   232‚Üí			if got != tt.want {
   233‚Üí				t.Errorf("NormalizePath(%q) = %q, want %q", tt.path, got, tt.want)
   234‚Üí			}
   235‚Üí		})
   236‚Üí	}
   237‚Üí}
```

```text
    30‚Üí// SessionData is the unified data format for sessions from all terminal coding agent providers

    97‚Üí// Validate checks the SessionData against schema constraints and logs warnings for any issues.
    98‚Üí// Returns true if validation passed, false if there were any issues.
    99‚Üífunc (s *SessionData) Validate() bool {
   100‚Üí	valid := true
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí	// Check schema version
   103‚Üí	if s.SchemaVersion != "1.0" {
   104‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: schemaVersion must be '1.0'", "got", s.SchemaVersion)
   105‚Üí		valid = false
   106‚Üí	}
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí	// Check required root fields
   109‚Üí	if s.Provider.ID == "" {
   110‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.id is required")
   111‚Üí		valid = false
   112‚Üí	}
   113‚Üí	if s.Provider.Name == "" {
   114‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.name is required")
   115‚Üí		valid = false
   116‚Üí	}
   117‚Üí	if s.Provider.Version == "" {
   118‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: provider.version is required")
   119‚Üí		valid = false
   120‚Üí	}
   121‚Üí	if s.SessionID == "" {
   122‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: sessionId is required")
   123‚Üí		valid = false
   124‚Üí	}
   125‚Üí	if s.CreatedAt == "" {
   126‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: createdAt is required")
   127‚Üí		valid = false
   128‚Üí	}
   129‚Üí	if s.WorkspaceRoot == "" {
   130‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: workspaceRoot is required")
   131‚Üí		valid = false
   132‚Üí	}
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí	// Validate each exchange
   135‚Üí	for i, exchange := range s.Exchanges {
   136‚Üí		if exchange.ExchangeID == "" {
   137‚Üí			slog.Warn("schema validation: exchange.exchangeId is required", "exchangeIndex", i)
   138‚Üí			valid = false
   139‚Üí		}
   140‚Üí
   141‚Üí		// Validate each message in the exchange
   142‚Üí		for j, msg := range exchange.Messages {
   143‚Üí			if !msg.validate(i, j) {
   144‚Üí				valid = false
   145‚Üí			}
   146‚Üí		}
   147‚Üí	}
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí	return valid
   150‚Üí}
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí// validate checks a single message against schema constraints.
   153‚Üífunc (m *Message) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex int) bool {
   154‚Üí	valid := true
   155‚Üí	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex}
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí	// Check role is valid
   158‚Üí	if m.Role != RoleUser && m.Role != RoleAgent {
   159‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: message.role must be 'user' or 'agent'",
   160‚Üí			append(logCtx, "got", m.Role)...)
   161‚Üí		valid = false
   162‚Üí	}
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí	// User message constraints
   165‚Üí	if m.Role == RoleUser {
   166‚Üí		if len(m.Content) == 0 {
   167‚Üí			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message must have non-empty content", logCtx...)
   168‚Üí			valid = false
   169‚Üí		}
   170‚Üí		if m.Tool != nil {
   171‚Üí			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message cannot have tool", logCtx...)
   172‚Üí			valid = false
   173‚Üí		}
   174‚Üí		if m.Model != "" {
   175‚Üí			slog.Warn("schema validation: user message cannot have model", logCtx...)
   176‚Üí			valid = false
   177‚Üí		}
   178‚Üí	}
   179‚Üí
   180‚Üí	// Agent message constraints: must have at least one of content, tool, or pathHints
   181‚Üí	if m.Role == RoleAgent {
   182‚Üí		hasContent := len(m.Content) > 0
   183‚Üí		hasTool := m.Tool != nil
   184‚Üí		hasPathHints := len(m.PathHints) > 0
   185‚Üí		if !hasContent && !hasTool && !hasPathHints {
   186‚Üí			slog.Warn("schema validation: agent message must have content, tool, or pathHints", logCtx...)
   187‚Üí			valid = false
   188‚Üí		}
   189‚Üí	}
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí	// Validate content parts
   192‚Üí	for k, part := range m.Content {
   193‚Üí		if !part.validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex, k) {
   194‚Üí			valid = false
   195‚Üí		}
   196‚Üí	}
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí	// Validate tool if present
   199‚Üí	if m.Tool != nil {
   200‚Üí		if !m.Tool.validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex) {
   201‚Üí			valid = false
   202‚Üí		}
   203‚Üí	}
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí	return valid
   206‚Üí}
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí// validate checks a single content part against schema constraints.
   209‚Üífunc (c *ContentPart) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex, partIndex int) bool {
   210‚Üí	valid := true
   211‚Üí	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex, "partIndex", partIndex}
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üí	if c.Type != ContentTypeText && c.Type != ContentTypeThinking {
   214‚Üí		slog.Warn("schema validation: contentPart.type must be 'text' or 'thinking'",
   215‚Üí			append(logCtx, "got", c.Type)...)
   216‚Üí		valid = false
   217‚Üí	}
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí	return valid
   220‚Üí}
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí// validate checks tool info against schema constraints.
   223‚Üífunc (t *ToolInfo) validate(exchangeIndex, messageIndex int) bool {
   224‚Üí	valid := true
   225‚Üí	logCtx := []any{"exchangeIndex", exchangeIndex, "messageIndex", messageIndex}
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üí	if t.Name == "" {

   229‚Üí		valid = false
   230‚Üí	}
   231‚Üí
   232‚Üí	validTypes := map[string]bool{
   233‚Üí		ToolTypeWrite:   true,

   235‚Üí		ToolTypeSearch:  true,

   244‚Üí		valid = false
   245‚Üí	}

   247‚Üí	return valid
```

```text
    11‚Üí	Success      bool   // Whether the check succeeded
    12‚Üí	Version      string // Version of the provider (empty on failure)
    13‚Üí	Location     string // File path/location of the provider executable
    14‚Üí	ErrorMessage string // Error message if check failed (empty on success)
    15‚Üí}
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí// ProgressCallback reports progress during session parsing/processing
    18‚Üí// current: number of items processed so far (1-based)
    19‚Üí// total: total number of items to process
    20‚Üítype ProgressCallback func(current, total int)
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí// AgentChatSession represents a chat session from an AI coding agent
    23‚Üítype AgentChatSession struct {
    24‚Üí	SessionID   string              // Stable and unique identifier for the session (often a UUID)
    25‚Üí	CreatedAt   string              // Stable ISO 8601 timestamp when session was created
    26‚Üí	Slug        string              // Stable human-readable but file name safe slug, often derived from first user message
    27‚Üí	SessionData *schema.SessionData // Structured session data in unified format
    28‚Üí	RawData     string              // Raw session data (e.g., JSON blobs for Cursor CLI, JSONL for Claude Code and Codex CLI, etc.)
    29‚Üí}
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí// SessionMetadata contains lightweight metadata about a session without full content
    32‚Üí// Used by ListAgentChatSessions for efficient session listing
    33‚Üítype SessionMetadata struct {
    34‚Üí	SessionID string `json:"session_id" csv:"session_id"` // Stable and unique identifier for the session
    35‚Üí	CreatedAt string `json:"created_at" csv:"created_at"` // Stable ISO 8601 timestamp when session was created
    36‚Üí	Slug      string `json:"slug" csv:"slug"`             // Stable human-readable session name/slug
    37‚Üí	Name      string `json:"name" csv:"name"`             // Human-readable description of the session (may be empty if not available)
    38‚Üí}
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí// Provider defines the interface that all agent coding tool providers must implement
    41‚Üítype Provider interface {
    42‚Üí	// Name returns the human-readable name of the provider (e.g., "Claude Code", "Cursor CLI", "Codex CLI")
    43‚Üí	Name() string
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí	// Check verifies if the provider is properly installed and returns version info
    46‚Üí	// customCommand: empty string = use detected/default binary path, non-empty = use this specific command/path
    47‚Üí	Check(customCommand string) CheckResult
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí	// DetectAgent checks if the provider's agent has been used in the given project path
    50‚Üí	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    51‚Üí	// helpOutput: if true AND no activity is found, the provider should output helpful guidance
    52‚Üí	// Returns true if the agent has created artifacts/sessions in the specified path
    53‚Üí	DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí	// GetAgentChatSession retrieves a single chat session by ID for the given project path
    56‚Üí	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    57‚Üí	// sessionID: specific session identifier to retrieve (always provided, never empty)
    58‚Üí	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    59‚Üí	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    60‚Üí	// Returns nil if the session is not found, error for actual errors
    61‚Üí	GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí	// GetAgentChatSessions retrieves all chat sessions for the given project path
    64‚Üí	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    65‚Üí	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    66‚Üí	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    67‚Üí	// progress: optional callback for reporting progress during parsing (nil = no progress reporting)
    68‚Üí	// Returns a slice of AgentChatSession structs containing session data
    69‚Üí	GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress ProgressCallback) ([]AgentChatSession, error)
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí	// ListAgentChatSessions retrieves lightweight metadata for all sessions without full parsing
    72‚Üí	// This should be faster than GetAgentChatSessions as it only needs to return SessionMetadata and not the full AgentChatSession
    73‚Üí	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    74‚Üí	// Returns a slice of SessionMetadata (ordering is provider-defined; consumers will sort if needed)
    75‚Üí	ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath string) ([]SessionMetadata, error)
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes the agent in interactive mode and watches for session updates
    78‚Üí	// Blocks until the agent exits, calling sessionCallback for each new/updated session
    79‚Üí	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    80‚Üí	// customCommand: empty string = use detected/default binary with default args, non-empty = use this specific command/path
    81‚Üí	// resumeSessionID: empty string = start new session, non-empty = resume this specific session ID
    82‚Üí	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    83‚Üí	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    84‚Üí	// sessionCallback is called with each session update (provider should not block on callback)
    85‚Üí	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking
    86‚Üí	ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí	// WatchAgent watches for agent activity and calls the callback with an updated AgentChatSession
    89‚Üí	// Does NOT execute the agent - only watches for new activity
    90‚Üí	// Runs until error or context cancellation
    91‚Üí	// ctx: Context for cancellation and timeout control
    92‚Üí	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
    93‚Üí	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
    94‚Üí	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
    95‚Üí	// sessionCallback: called with AgentChatSession on each update (provider should not block on callback)
    96‚Üí	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking
    97‚Üí	WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error
    98‚Üí}
    99‚Üí
```

```text
    57‚Üí// startProvenanceEngine creates and returns a provenance engine if --provenance is enabled.
    58‚Üí// Returns nil engine and nil cleanup if provenance is not enabled.
    59‚Üí// The caller must invoke the returned cleanup function (typically via defer).
    60‚Üífunc startProvenanceEngine() (*provenance.Engine, func(), error) {
    61‚Üí	if !provenanceEnabled {
    62‚Üí		return nil, func() {}, nil
    63‚Üí	}
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí	engine, err := provenance.NewEngine()
    66‚Üí	if err != nil {
    67‚Üí		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance engine: %w", err)
    68‚Üí	}
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí	cleanup := func() {
    71‚Üí		if closeErr := engine.Close(); closeErr != nil {
    72‚Üí			slog.Error("Failed to close provenance engine", "error", closeErr)
    73‚Üí		}
    74‚Üí		slog.Info("Provenance engine stopped")
    75‚Üí	}
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí	return engine, cleanup, nil
    78‚Üí}
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí// startProvenanceFSWatcher creates a filesystem watcher that pushes FileEvents
    81‚Üí// to the provenance engine for correlation with agent activity. Returns a cleanup
    82‚Üí// function the caller must invoke (typically via defer). If the engine is nil
    83‚Üí// (provenance disabled), returns a no-op cleanup.
    84‚Üífunc startProvenanceFSWatcher(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, rootDir string) (func(), error) {
    85‚Üí	if engine == nil {
    86‚Üí		return func() {}, nil
    87‚Üí	}
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí	watcher, err := provenance.NewFSWatcher(engine, rootDir)
    90‚Üí	if err != nil {
    91‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance FS watcher: %w", err)
    92‚Üí	}
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí	watcher.Start(ctx)
    95‚Üí	return watcher.Stop, nil
    96‚Üí}
    97‚Üí

    99‚Üí// to the provenance engine. Safe to call with nil engine (no-op).
   100‚Üífunc processProvenanceEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
```

```text
   100‚Üífunc processProvenanceEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, session *spi.AgentChatSession) {

   108‚Üívar uuidRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$`)
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí// pluralSession returns "session" or "sessions" based on count for proper grammar
   111‚Üífunc pluralSession(count int) string {
   112‚Üí	if count == 1 {
   113‚Üí		return "session"
   114‚Üí	}
   115‚Üí	return "sessions"
   116‚Üí}
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí// SyncStats tracks the results of a sync operation
   119‚Üítype SyncStats struct {
   120‚Üí	TotalSessions   int
   121‚Üí	SessionsSkipped int // Already up to date
   122‚Üí	SessionsUpdated int // Existed but needed update
   123‚Üí	SessionsCreated int // Newly created markdown files
   124‚Üí}
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí// validateFlags checks for mutually exclusive flag combinations
   127‚Üífunc validateFlags() error {
   128‚Üí	if console && silent {
   129‚Üí		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "cannot use --console and --silent together. These flags are mutually exclusive"}
   130‚Üí	}
   131‚Üí	if debug && !console && !logFile {
   132‚Üí		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--debug requires either --console or --log to be specified"}
   133‚Üí	}
   134‚Üí	if onlyCloudSync && noCloudSync {
   135‚Üí		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "cannot use --only-cloud-sync and --no-cloud-sync together. These flags are mutually exclusive"}
   136‚Üí	}
   137‚Üí	if printToStdout && onlyCloudSync {
   138‚Üí		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "cannot use --print and --only-cloud-sync together. These flags are mutually exclusive"}
   139‚Üí	}
   140‚Üí	if printToStdout && console {
   141‚Üí		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "cannot use --print and --console together. Console debug output would interleave with markdown on stdout"}
   142‚Üí	}
   143‚Üí	return nil
   144‚Üí}
   145‚Üí
   146‚Üí// validateUUID checks if the given string is a valid UUID format
   147‚Üífunc validateUUID(uuid string) bool {
   148‚Üí	return uuidRegex.MatchString(uuid)
   149‚Üí}
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí// getUseUTC reads the local-time-zone flag and converts it to useUTC format.
   152‚Üí// When --local-time-zone is false (default), useUTC is true.
   153‚Üífunc getUseUTC(cmd *cobra.Command) bool {
   154‚Üí	useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
   155‚Üí	return !useLocalTimezone
   156‚Üí}
   157‚Üí
   158‚Üífunc checkAndWarnAuthentication() {
   159‚Üí	if !noCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() && !silent {
   160‚Üí		// Check if this was due to a 401 authentication failure
   161‚Üí		if cloud.HadAuthFailure() {
   162‚Üí			// Show the specific message for auth failures with orange warning and emoji
   163‚Üí			slog.Warn("Cloud sync authentication failed (401)")
   164‚Üí			log.UserWarn("‚öÝÔ∏è Unable to authenticate with SpecStory Cloud. This could be due to revoked or expired credentials, or network/server issues.\n")
   165‚Üí			log.UserMessage("‚ÑπÔ∏è If this persists, run `specstory logout` then `specstory login` to reset your SpecStory Cloud authentication.\n")
   166‚Üí		} else {
   167‚Üí			// Regular "not authenticated" message
   168‚Üí			msg := "‚öÝÔ∏è Cloud sync not available. You're not authenticated."
   169‚Üí			slog.Warn(msg)
   170‚Üí			log.UserWarn("%s\n", msg)
   171‚Üí			log.UserMessage("‚ÑπÔ∏è Use `specstory login` to authenticate, or `--no-cloud-sync` to skip this warning.\n")
   172‚Üí		}
   173‚Üí	}
   174‚Üí}
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí// createRootCommand dynamically creates the root command with provider information
   177‚Üífunc createRootCommand() *cobra.Command {
   178‚Üí	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
   179‚Üí	ids := registry.ListIDs()
   180‚Üí	providerList := registry.GetProviderList()
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí	// Build dynamic examples based on registered providers
   183‚Üí	examples := `
   184‚Üí# Check terminal coding agents installation
   185‚Üíspecstory check
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí# Run the default agent with auto-saving
   188‚Üíspecstory run`
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí	// Add provider-specific examples if we have providers
   191‚Üí	if len(ids) > 0 {
   192‚Üí		examples += "\n\n# Run a specific agent with auto-saving"
   193‚Üí		for _, id := range ids {

   195‚Üí				examples += fmt.Sprintf("\nspecstory run %s", id)
   196‚Üí			}
   197‚Üí		}
   198‚Üí
   199‚Üí		// Use first provider for custom command example
   200‚Üí		examples += fmt.Sprintf("\n\n# Run with custom command\nspecstory run %s -c \"/custom/path/to/agent\"", ids[0])
   201‚Üí	}
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí	examples += `
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí# Generate markdown files for all agent sessions associated with the current directory
   206‚Üíspecstory sync
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí# Generate markdown files for specific agent sessions
   209‚Üíspecstory sync -s <session-id>
   210‚Üíspecstory sync -s <session-id-1> -s <session-id-2>
   211‚Üí
   212‚Üí# Watch for any agent activity in the current directory and generate markdown files
   213‚Üíspecstory watch`
   214‚Üí
   215‚Üí	longDesc := `SpecStory is a wrapper for terminal coding agents that auto-saves markdown files of all your chat interactions.`
   216‚Üí	if providerList != "No providers registered" {
   217‚Üí		longDesc += "\n\nSupported agents: " + providerList + "."
   218‚Üí	}
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí	return &cobra.Command{
   221‚Üí		Use:               "specstory [command]",
   222‚Üí		Short:             "SpecStory auto-saves terminal coding agent chat interactions",
   223‚Üí		Long:              longDesc,
   224‚Üí		Example:           examples,
   225‚Üí		SilenceUsage:      true,
   226‚Üí		SilenceErrors:     true,
   227‚Üí		DisableAutoGenTag: true,
   228‚Üí		CompletionOptions: cobra.CompletionOptions{DisableDefaultCmd: true},
   229‚Üí		PersistentPreRunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
   230‚Üí			// Validate flags before any other setup
   231‚Üí			if err := validateFlags(); err != nil {
   232‚Üí				fmt.Println() // Add visual separation before error message for better CLI readability
   233‚Üí				return err
   234‚Üí			}
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí			// Configure logging based on flags
   237‚Üí			if console || logFile {
   238‚Üí				// Create output config to get proper log path if needed
   239‚Üí				var logPath string
   240‚Üí				if logFile {
   241‚Üí					config, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(outputDir)
   242‚Üí					if err != nil {
   243‚Üí						return err
   244‚Üí					}
   245‚Üí					logPath = config.GetLogPath()
   246‚Üí				}
   247‚Üí
   248‚Üí				// Set up logger
   249‚Üí				if err := log.SetupLogger(console, logFile, debug, logPath); err != nil {
   250‚Üí					return fmt.Errorf("failed to set up logger: %v", err)
   251‚Üí				}
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üí				// Log startup information
   254‚Üí				slog.Info("=== SpecStory Starting ===")
   255‚Üí				slog.Info("Version", "version", version)
   256‚Üí				slog.Info("Command line", "args", strings.Join(os.Args, " "))
   257‚Üí				if cwd, err := os.Getwd(); err == nil {
   258‚Üí					slog.Info("Current working directory", "cwd", cwd)
   259‚Üí				}
   260‚Üí				slog.Info("========================")
   261‚Üí			} else {
   262‚Üí				// No logging - set up discard logger
   263‚Üí				if err := log.SetupLogger(false, false, false, ""); err != nil {
   264‚Üí					return err
   265‚Üí				}
   266‚Üí			}
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí			// Set silent mode for user messages
   269‚Üí			log.SetSilent(silent)
   270‚Üí
   271‚Üí			// Initialize cloud sync manager
   272‚Üí			cloud.InitSyncManager(!noCloudSync)
   273‚Üí			cloud.SetSilent(silent)
   274‚Üí			cloud.SetClientVersion(version)
   275‚Üí			// Set custom cloud URL if provided (otherwise cloud package uses its default)
   276‚Üí			if cloudURL != "" {
   277‚Üí				cloud.SetAPIBaseURL(cloudURL)
   278‚Üí			}
   279‚Üí
   280‚Üí			// If --cloud-token flag was provided, verify the refresh token works
   281‚Üí			// This bypasses normal authentication and uses the token for this session only
   282‚Üí			if cloudToken != "" {

   284‚Üí				if err := cloud.SetSessionRefreshToken(cloudToken); err != nil {
   285‚Üí					fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr) // Visual separation

   287‚Üí					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "   %v\n", err)

   290‚Üí					fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "   Please check your token and try again, or use 'specstory login' for interactive authentication.")
   291‚Üí					fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr)
```

```text
    23‚Üífunc TestSessionData_Validate(t *testing.T) {
    24‚Üí	tests := []struct {

    29‚Üí		{

    33‚Üí		},
    34‚Üí		{
    35‚Üí			name:     "valid session with empty exchanges array",
    36‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.Exchanges = []Exchange{} },

    38‚Üí		},
    39‚Üí		{
    40‚Üí			name: "valid session with exchanges",
    41‚Üí			modify: func(s *SessionData) {

    43‚Üí					{
    44‚Üí						ExchangeID: "exchange-1",
    45‚Üí						Messages: []Message{
    46‚Üí							{Role: RoleUser, Content: []ContentPart{{Type: ContentTypeText, Text: "Hello"}}},
    47‚Üí						},
    48‚Üí					},
    49‚Üí				}
    50‚Üí			},
    51‚Üí			expected: true,
    52‚Üí		},
    53‚Üí		{
    54‚Üí			name:     "missing schemaVersion",
    55‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.SchemaVersion = "" },
    56‚Üí			expected: false,
    57‚Üí		},
    58‚Üí		{
    59‚Üí			name:     "wrong schemaVersion",
    60‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.SchemaVersion = "2.0" },
    61‚Üí			expected: false,
    62‚Üí		},
    63‚Üí		{
    64‚Üí			name:     "missing provider.id",
    65‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.Provider.ID = "" },
    66‚Üí			expected: false,
    67‚Üí		},
    68‚Üí		{
    69‚Üí			name:     "missing provider.name",
    70‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.Provider.Name = "" },
    71‚Üí			expected: false,
    72‚Üí		},
    73‚Üí		{
    74‚Üí			name:     "missing provider.version",
    75‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.Provider.Version = "" },
    76‚Üí			expected: false,
    77‚Üí		},
    78‚Üí		{
    79‚Üí			name:     "missing sessionId",
    80‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.SessionID = "" },
    81‚Üí			expected: false,
    82‚Üí		},
    83‚Üí		{
    84‚Üí			name:     "missing createdAt",
    85‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.CreatedAt = "" },
    86‚Üí			expected: false,
    87‚Üí		},
    88‚Üí		{
    89‚Üí			name:     "missing workspaceRoot",
    90‚Üí			modify:   func(s *SessionData) { s.WorkspaceRoot = "" },
    91‚Üí			expected: false,
    92‚Üí		},
    93‚Üí		{
    94‚Üí			name: "multiple missing fields",
    95‚Üí			modify: func(s *SessionData) {
    96‚Üí				s.SchemaVersion = ""
    97‚Üí				s.SessionID = ""
    98‚Üí				s.Provider.ID = ""
    99‚Üí			},
   100‚Üí			expected: false,
   101‚Üí		},
   102‚Üí		{

   109‚Üí	}
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí	for _, tt := range tests {
```

```text
    73‚Üífunc TestShouldWatch_Gitignore(t *testing.T) {
    74‚Üí	// Create a temp directory with a .gitignore
    75‚Üí	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí	gitignoreContent := "build/\n*.generated.go\noutput.txt\n"

    79‚Üí		t.Fatal(err)

   260‚Üífunc TestFSWatcher_Debounce(t *testing.T) {
   261‚Üí	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
   262‚Üí
   263‚Üí	dbPath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "test-provenance.db")

   267‚Üí	}

   269‚Üí
   270‚Üí	w, err := NewFSWatcher(engine, tmpDir)
   271‚Üí	if err != nil {
   272‚Üí		t.Fatal(err)
   273‚Üí	}
   274‚Üí
   275‚Üí	ctx := context.Background()
   276‚Üí	w.Start(ctx)
   277‚Üí	defer w.Stop()
   278‚Üí
   279‚Üí	// Create a file, then write to it again rapidly
   280‚Üí	testFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "rapid.txt")
   281‚Üí	if err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte("first"), 0o644); err != nil {
   282‚Üí		t.Fatal(err)
   283‚Üí	}
   284‚Üí
   285‚Üí	// Write again immediately (within debounce window)
   286‚Üí	if err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte("second"), 0o644); err != nil {
   287‚Üí		t.Fatal(err)
   288‚Üí	}
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí	// Give fsnotify time to process
   291‚Üí	time.Sleep(300 * time.Millisecond)
   292‚Üí
   293‚Üí	// Query file events ‚Äî debouncing should have collapsed rapid writes
   294‚Üí	since := time.Now().Add(-10 * time.Second)
   295‚Üí	until := time.Now().Add(10 * time.Second)
   296‚Üí	events, err := engine.store.QueryUnmatchedFileEvents(ctx, since, until)
   297‚Üí	if err != nil {
   298‚Üí		t.Fatal(err)
   299‚Üí	}
   300‚Üí
   301‚Üí	// Count events for our test file
   302‚Üí	count := 0
   303‚Üí	for _, e := range events {
   304‚Üí		if e.FilePath == testFile {
   305‚Üí			count++
   306‚Üí		}
   307‚Üí	}
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí	// We should have exactly 1 event (create), with the rapid write debounced away
   310‚Üí	if count != 1 {
   311‚Üí		t.Errorf("expected 1 file event after debouncing, got %d", count)
   312‚Üí	}
   313‚Üí}
```
